1.1: Because many were willing to write an account of those events of which we are persuaded,
1.2: Like the things which they delivered to us who from the first were eyewitnesses and servants of The Word,
1.3: He appeared also to me because I had approached all things carefully, that I would write everything in order to you, excellent Theophila.
1.4: That you may know the truth of the words of which you have been instructed by such.
1.5: In the days of Herodus, The King of Judea, there was a certain Priest whose name was Zechariah from the Ministry of the house of Abia and his wife was named Elizabeth who was of the daughters of Aaron.
1.6: Both of them were righteous before God, and they were walking in all the commandments and in the judgments of THE LORD JEHOVAH without reproa
1.7: But they did not have a son, because Elizabeth was infertile, and both of them were advanced in their days.
1.8: And as he was exercising his priestly function in the order of his service before God,
1.9: In the tradition of the priesthood, it befell him to offer incense, and he entered The Temple of THE LORD JEHOVAH.
1.10: And all the crowds of the people were praying outside at the time of incense.
1.11: And an Angel of THE LORD JEHOVAH appeared to Zechariah who stood at the right side of the altar of incense.
1.12: And Zechariah was troubled when he saw him and dread fell upon him.
1.13: And the Angel said to him, “Do not be afraid, Zacharia, because your prayer has been heard and your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you shall call his name, Yohannan.”
1.14: “You will have joy and gladness and many will rejoice at his birth.”
1.15: “He will be great before THE LORD JEHOVAH, and he will not drink wine or strong drink, and he will be filled with The Spirit of Holiness while he is in the womb of his mother.”
1.16: “And he will turn many of the children of Israel to THE LORD JEHOVAH, their God.”
1.17: “And he will go before him in the spirit and in the power of Elijah The Prophet to turn the heart of The fathers to the children and those who do not have faith to the knowledge of The Just One and he will prepare a perfect people for THE LORD JEHOVAH.”
1.18: And Zacharia said to the Angel, “How shall I know this, for I am old and my wife is advanced in her days?”
1.19: The Angel answered and he said to him, “I am Gabriel who am standing before God, and I am sent to speak with you and to give you these tidings.”
1.20: “Henceforth, you will be dumb, and you will not be able to speak until the day that these things will occur, because you did not believe my words, which will be fulfilled in their time.”
1.21: But the people were standing and waiting for Zacharia, and they were wondering at his tarrying in The Temple.
1.22: But when Zechariah came out, he was not able to speak with them, and they perceived that he had seen a vision in The Temple and was making signs, gesturing to them, and he still remained mute.
1.23: And when the days of his service were fulfilled he went to his house.
1.24: And it happened after those days, Elizabeth his wife conceived and she had secluded herself for five months, and she said:
1.25: “THE LORD JEHOVAH has done these things for me in the days in which he regarded me to take away my reproach among the children of men”
1.26: But in the six month, Gabriel the Angel was sent from the presence of God to Galilee to the city whose name was Nazareth,
1.27: To a virgin who was engaged to a man from the house of David, whose name was Yoseph, and the name of the virgin was Maryam.
1.28: The Angel entered her presence, and he said to her, “Peace to you, full of grace, our Lord is with you; you are blessed among women.”
1.29: But when she saw him, she was alarmed at his word, and she was wondering, “What is this greeting?”
1.30: And the Angel said to her, “Do not fear, Maryam, for you have found favor with God,
1.31: For behold, you shall conceive and you shall give birth to a son, and you shall call his name Yeshua.
1.32: This One will be great and he will be called The Son of The Highest and THE LORD JEHOVAH will give to him the throne of David his father,
1.33: And he will reign over the house of Jaqob eternally and to his Kingdom there will not be an end.”
1.34: Maryam said to the Angel, “How will this be, for no man has known me?”
1.35: The Angel answered and said to her, “The Spirit of Holiness shall come and the power of The Highest shall rest upon you, therefore he who shall be begotten in you shall be holy and he shall be called the Son of God.”
1.36: “And behold, Elizabeth your cousin also has conceived a son in her old age, and this is the sixth month for her, who is called barren,”
1.38: Maryam said, “Behold, I am the handmaid of THE LORD JEHOVAH; let it be done to me according to your word”, and the Angel departed from her.
1.39: But Maryam arose in those days, and she went instantly to the mountains to a city of Judea.
1.40: And she entered the house of Zechariah, and she invoked the peace of Elizabeth.
1.41: And it was when Elizabeth heard the greeting of Maryam, the baby in her womb leaped and Elizabeth was filled with The Spirit of Holiness,
1.42: And she cried with a loud voice and she said to Maryam, “You are blessed among women and blessed is the fruit that is in your womb.”
1.43: “From where is this to me that the mother of my Lord would come to me?”
1.44: “For behold, when the voice of your greeting fell on my ear, the baby in my womb leaped for great joy.”
1.45: “And blessed is she who believed that there would be a fulfillment to those things that were spoken with her from the presence of THE LORD JEHOVAH.”
1.46: And Maryam said, “My soul exalts THE LORD JEHOVAH”
1.47: “And my spirit rejoices in God my Savior.”
1.48: “For he has regarded the lowliness of his Maidservant, for behold, from this hour all generations will ascribe blessedness to me.”
1.49: “Because he who is mighty has done unto me great things and holy is his name.”
1.50: “And his mercy for posterity and generations is upon those who revere him.”
1.51: “He has wrought victory with his arm and he has scattered the proud with the opinion of their heart.”
1.52: “He has cast down the mighty from thrones and has raised up the lowly.”
1.53: “He has filled the hungry with good things and the rich he has sent away empty handed.”
1.54: “He has helped Israel his servant and he has remembered his mercy,”
1.55: “Just as he spoke with our Patriarchs, with Abraham and with his seed eternally.”
1.56: But Maryam stayed with Elizabeth about three months, and she returned to her house.
1.57: But it came time for Elizabeth to give birth, and she bore a son.
1.58: And her neighbors heard, and the members of her family, that God had magnified his mercy to her, and they rejoiced with her.
1.59: And it was the eighth day, and they came to circumcise the boy, and they were calling him by the name of his father Zachariah.
1.60: And his mother answered, and she said to them, “Not so, but he will be called Yohannan.”
1.61: And they said to her, “There is no man among your generations who is called by this name.”
1.62: And they signed to his father how he wished to name him.
1.63: He requested a tablet and he wrote and he said, “Yohannan is his name”, and everyone was amazed.
1.64: And at once his mouth was opened, and his tongue, and he spoke and blessed God.
1.65: And there was awe upon all of their neighbors, and in all the mountains of Judea these things were being spoken.
1.66: And all those who heard were contemplating in their heart and they were saying, “What indeed will this boy be?” And the hand of THE LORD JEHOVAH was with him.
1.67: And his father Zechariah was filled with The Spirit of Holiness and he prophesied and he said:
1.68: “Blessed is THE LORD JEHOVAH, The God of Israel, who has visited his nation, and he has wrought for it redemption.”
1.69: “And he has raised up for us a trumpet of redemption in the house of David his servant,”
1.70: “Just as he spoke by the mouth of his holy Prophets that which is from eternity,”
1.71: “That he would save us from our enemies and from the hand of all of them who hate us.”
1.72: “And he has wrought his mercy with our fathers and he has remembered his holy covenants”
1.73: “And the oath that he swore to Abraham our Patriarch that he would grant us”
1.74: “That we would be saved from the hands of our enemies, and we would serve before him without fear,”
1.75: “All of our days with justice and in righteousness.”
1.76: “And you, boy, you will be called the Prophet of The Highest, for you will go before the face of THE LORD JEHOVAH that you may prepare his way,”
1.77: “That he may give the knowledge of life to his people in forgiveness of their sins,”
1.78: “In the compassions of the mercy of our God in which The Manifestation from Heaven will visit us,”
1.79: “To enlighten those who were sitting in darkness and in the shadow of death, that he may direct our feet in the path of peace.”
1.80: But the boy was growing and being strengthened in spirit, and he dwelt in the wilderness until the day of his manifestation unto Israel.

2.1: But it occurred in those days that a command also went out from Augustus Caesar that every nation of his empire would be registered.
2.2: This census was the first in the government of Quraynus in Syria.
2.3: And everyone was going to be registered in his own city.
2.4: But Yoseph had come up from Nazareth, a city of Galilee, to Judea to the city of David called Bethlehem, because he was from the house and from the lineage of David,
2.5: With Maryam his bride when she was pregnant, that he might be registered there.
2.6: And it was, that while they were there, the days were completed for her to give birth.
2.7: And she brought forth her firstborn Son and she wrapped him in swaddling bands, and she laid him in a manger because there was no place for them where they might lodge.
2.8: But there were some shepherds in the region lodging there and they kept the nightly watch over their flocks;
2.9: And behold, the Angel of God came to them and the glory of THE LORD JEHOVAH shone upon them and they were greatly afraid;
2.10: And the Angel said to them, “Do not be afraid, for behold, I proclaim to you great joy which will be to the entire universe,”
2.11: “For today, The Savior has been born to you, who is THE LORD JEHOVAH The Messiah, in the city of David.”
2.12: “And this is a sign for you: You will find The Baby wrapped in swaddling bands and lying in a manger.”
2.13: And suddenly, the great armies of Heaven appeared with the Angel, while shouting praises to God, and they were saying:
2.14: “Glory to God in Heaven, and upon earth peace, Good News to the children of men.”
2.15: It was that as the Angels went from them into Heaven, the shepherds spoke one with another and they were saying, “Let us go as far as Bethlehem, and we shall see this event that has occurred as THE LORD JEHOVAH has revealed to us.”
2.16: And they came quickly and they found Maryam and Yoseph and The Baby who was lying in a manger.
2.17: “And when they had seen, they made known the words that had been spoken with them about The Boy.”
2.18: And all who heard marveled concerning those things that were spoken to them by the shepherds.
2.19: But Maryam was keeping all these words and was pondering in her heart.
2.20: And the shepherds returned as they glorified and praised God concerning everything, because they had seen and heard just as it had been spoken with them.
2.21: And when the eight days were complete that The Boy should be circumcised, his name was called Yeshua, which he was called by the Angel before he would have been conceived in the womb.
2.22: And when the days were fulfilled that they should be purified according to the law of Moses, they carried him to Jerusalem to present him before THE LORD JEHOVAH,
2.23: As it is written in the law of THE LORD JEHOVAH, “Every male who opens the womb will be called a holy one of THE LORD JEHOVAH,”
2.24: And to offer a sacrifice, just like that which was said in the law of THE LORD JEHOVAH, “A pair of turtle doves or two young doves.”
2.25: But one man was there in Jerusalem, whose name was Shimeon, and this man was just and righteous and he was waiting for the consolation of Israel and The Spirit of Holiness was upon him.
2.26: And it was told him by The Spirit of Holiness that he would not see death until he would see The Messiah of THE LORD JEHOVAH.
2.27: This one had come by The Spirit of Holiness to The Temple, and as his parents brought The Boy Yeshua to do for him just as it was commanded in the law,
2.28: He took him in his arms and he blessed God and said:
2.29: “Now send your servant in peace, my Lord, according to your word.”
2.30: “Behold, my eyes have seen your mercy,”
2.31: “Him whom you have prepared in the sight of all the peoples.”
2.32: “The Light for the revelation of the Gentiles and The Glory to your people Israel.”
2.33: But Yoseph and his mother were marveling at those things which were spoken about him.
2.34: And Shimeon blessed them and said to Maryam his mother, “Behold, This One is appointed for the fall and the rise of many in Israel and for a sign of contention.”
2.35: “And a lance will pass through into your soul, so that the thoughts of many hearts may be revealed.”
2.36: And Hannah the Prophetess, daughter of Phanuel from the tribe of Asher, she was also in her old age, and had lived with her husband seven years after her virginity.
2.37: And she had been a widow about 84 years, and she had not departed from The Temple, and with fastings and with prayers she was serving by day and night.
2.38: She was also standing in it at that hour and she gave thanks to THE LORD JEHOVAH and was speaking about him with everyone who was waiting for the redemption of Jerusalem.
2.39: And when they had finished everything according to that which is in the law of THE LORD JEHOVAH, they returned to Galilee to Nazareth their city.
2.40: But The Boy was growing and being strengthened in spirit and he was filled with wisdom and the grace of God was upon him.
2.41: And every year his people were going to Jerusalem at the feast of Passover.
2.42: And when he was twelve years old, they went out to the feast just as they had been accustomed;
2.43: And when the days were past, they were returning, but The Boy Yeshua had remained in Jerusalem and Yoseph and his mother did not know it.
2.44: For they were thinking that he was with the children of their friends, and when they had come a journey of one day, they looked for him among their people and among whoever knew them,
2.45: And they did not find him, and they returned again to Jerusalem and they were looking for him.
2.46: After three days, they found him in The Temple as he sat in the midst of the Teachers and he heard from them and he was inquiring of them.
2.47: And all who were listening to him were astounded at his wisdom and at his discourses.
2.48: And when they saw him, they marveled, and his mother said to him, “My son, why have you done this to us? Look, your father and I were looking for you with great anxiety.”
2.49: He said to them, “Why were you looking for me? Do you not know that it was fitting for me to be in my Father's house?”
2.50: But they did not understand the statement that he spoke to them.
2.51: And he went down with them and he came to Nazareth, and he was submitted to them; but his mother was keeping all these words in her heart.
2.52: But Yeshua was growing in his stature and in his wisdom and in favor with God and the children of men.

3.1: But in the 15th year of the Kingdom of Tiberius Caesar in the government of Pontius Pilate in Judea, when Herodus was the Tetrarch in Galilee and Philippus his brother was Tetrarch in Ituria and in the region of Trakona, and Lusania was Tetrarch of Abilina,
3.2: During The High Priesthood of Khanan and of Qaiapha, the word of God came upon Yohannan, son of Zachariah, in the wilderness.
3.3: And he came into the whole region which is around the Jordan while he was preaching a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins,
3.4: Just as it is written in the book of the words of Isaiah the Prophet, which says, “The voice which cries in the wilderness, 'Prepare the way of THE LORD JEHOVAH and straighten in the plain a road for our God.”
3.5: “All the valleys will be filled and all the mountains and high places will be leveled and the rugged place will be smooth and the difficult region a plain,”
3.6: “And everybody will see The Life of God.”
3.7: And he said to the crowds who were coming to him to be baptized, “Offspring of Vipers! Who has instructed you to flee from the wrath that is coming?”
3.8: “Produce therefore fruits worthy for repentance and do not start to say within yourselves, 'Abraham is our father', for I say to you that God can raise up from these stones children to Abraham.”
3.9: “But behold, the ax is laid on the root of the tree. Every tree therefore, which is not producing good fruit is cut down and falls into the fire.”
3.10: And the crowds were asking him and they were saying, “What, therefore, shall we do?”
3.11: And he answered and he said to them, “Whoever has two coats, let him give to him who has not, and whoever has food should do likewise.”
3.12: And Tax Collectors came also to be baptized, and they were saying to him, “Teacher, what shall we do?”
3.13: But he said to them, “Do not require anything on top of whatever is commanded you to require.”
3.14: And soldiers were asking him and they were saying, “What shall we do also?” He said to them, “Do harm to no man, do no injustice and let your wages be sufficient for you.”
3.15: But as the people had been thinking about Yohannan and they were all pondering in their hearts whether he were The Messiah,
3.16: Yohannan answered and said to them, “Behold, I am baptizing you in water, but he comes after me, who is mightier than I, the straps of whose shoes I am not worthy to loose. He will baptize you in The Spirit of Holiness and in fire,”
3.17: “He who holds a winnowing fan in his hand and purges his threshing floor; the wheat he gathers into his barns and the chaff he will burn in unquenched fire.”
3.18: Also he taught many other things, and he preached The Good News to the people.
3.19: But Herodus the Tetrarch, because he had been reproved by Yohannan on the account of Herodia the wife of Philippus, his brother, and for all the evil that he had done,
3.20: He added this to all these things and shut Yohannan in prison.
3.21: It happened that when he had baptized all the people, he baptized Yeshua also, and as he prayed, the heavens were opened.
3.22: And The Spirit of Holiness was descending upon him in the form of a body of a dove, and there was a voice from Heaven, which said, “You are My Son, The Beloved, in whom I am delighted.”
3.23: But Yeshua was about thirty years old, and he was considered the son of Yoseph, son of Heli,
3.24: Son of Matthat, son of Levi, son of Melki, son of Yannai, son of Yoseph,
3.25: Son of Matatha, son of Amots, son of Nahum, son of Hesli, son of Naggai,
3.26: Son of Maath, son of Matath, son of Shimei, son of Yoseph, son of Yehudah,
3.27: Son of Yohannan, son of Resa, son of Zorobabel, son of Salathiel, son of Nari,
3.28: Son of Melki, son of Addi, son of Qosam, son of Elmodad, son of Ayr,
3.29: Son of Yose, son of Eliezer, son of Joram, son of Mathitha, son of Levi,
3.30: Son of Shimeon, son of Yehuda, son of Yoseph, son of Yonam, son of Eliakim,
3.31: Son of Melia, son of a Mainai, son of Matatha, son of Nathan, son of David,
3.32: Son of Ashai, son of Obed, son of Boaz, son of Salmon, son of Nahshon,
3.33: Son of Aminadab, son of Aram, son of Hetsron, son of Pharets, son of Yehudah,
3.34: Son of Jaqob, son of Isaaq, son of Abraham, son of Terah, son of Nahor,
3.35: Son of Serug, son of Arau, son of Phaleg, son of Eber, son of Shalah,
3.36: Son of Qainan, son of Arphakshar, son of Shaym, son of Noah, son of Lamek,
3.37: Son of Methuselah, son of Enoch, son of Jared, son of Mehalaleil, son of Qainan,
3.38: Son of Enosh, son of Shayth, son of Adam, who was from God.

4.1: But Yeshua, being full of The Spirit of Holiness, returned from the Jordan and The Spirit led him into the wilderness
4.2: To be tempted by The Devil for forty days, and he ate nothing in those days, and when he had finished them, at the end he was hungry.
4.3: And The Devil said to him, “If you are the Son of God, tell this stone to become bread.”
4.4: Yeshua answered and said to him, “It is written: a man does not live by bread alone, but by every utterance of God.”
4.5: And Satan took him up to a high mountain, and he showed him all the Kingdoms of the earth in a short time.
4.6: And The Devil said to him, “I shall give you all this dominion and its glory, for it is delivered to me, and I give it to whomever I want.”
4.7: “If therefore you will worship before me, it will all be yours.”
4.8: Yeshua answered and said to him, “It is written: 'you shall worship THE LORD JEHOVAH your God, and him only you shall serve.' “
4.9: And he brought him to Jerusalem and he stood him on the pinnacle of The Temple and he said to him, “If you are the Son of God, cast yourself down from here.”
4.10: “For it is written: 'He will command his Angels concerning you that they will protect you,' “
4.11: 'And they will carry you upon their arms, lest you should strike your foot on a stone.'”
4.12: But Yeshua answered and he said to him, “It has been said, 'You shall not tempt THE LORD JEHOVAH your God.' “
4.13: And when The Devil had finished all his temptations, he departed from his presence for a time.
4.14: And Yeshua returned in the power of The Spirit to Galilee and a report went out about him in every place around them.
4.15: And he was teaching in their assemblies and he was being praised by every person.
4.16: And he came to Nazareth where he had been raised, and as he was accustomed, he entered the synagogue on the Sabbath day, and he stood up to read.
4.17: And the scroll of Isaiah the Prophet was given to him and Yeshua opened the scroll and he found the place where it is written:
4.18: “The Spirit of THE LORD JEHOVAH is upon me, and because of this he has anointed me to preach The Good News to the poor; he has sent me to heal broken hearts and to proclaim liberty to captives, vision to the blind, and to restore the crushed with forgiveness,”
4.19: “And to proclaim the acceptable era of THE LORD JEHOVAH.”
4.20: And he rolled up the scroll and he gave it to the minister and he went and sat down, but all who were in the synagogue fixed their eyes upon him.
4.21: And he began to say to them, “Today this scripture is fulfilled in your ears.”
4.22: And all of them were testifying to it, and they were amazed at the words of grace that came out of his mouth. And they were saying, “Is not this Yoseph's son?”
4.23: Yeshua said to them, “Doubtless you will say to me this proverb: 'Physician, heal yourself', and 'Everything that we are hearing that you have done in Kapernahum, do also here in your town.'”
4.24: But he said, “Assuredly, I say to you, there is not a Prophet who is received in his town.”
4.25: “For truly I say to you that many widows were in Israel in the days of Elijah the Prophet, when the heavens were shut for three years and six months, and there was great hunger in all the land.”
4.26: “And Elijah was not sent to one of them except to Tsarepta of Tsidon unto a widow woman.”
4.27: “And there were many lepers among Israel in the days of the Elisha the Prophet and not one of them was purified except Naaman the Syrian.”
4.28: And when they who were in the synagogue heard these things, they were all filled with rage.
4.29: And they arose and they thrust him outside of the city and they brought him unto the ridge of the mountain on which the city was built to cast him from the precipice.
4.30: But he passed through the midst of them and he departed.
4.31: And he went down to Kapernahum, a city of Galilee, and he was teaching them on the Sabbath.
4.32: And they were astonished at his teaching for his message was with authority.
4.33: And there was a man in the synagogue who had the spirit of a filthy demon in him and he cried with a loud voice
4.34: And he said, “Let me alone! What business do we have with you, Yeshua Nazarene? Have you come to destroy us? I know you, who you are, The Holy One of God!”
4.35: And Yeshua rebuked it and he said, “Shut your mouth and come out of him!” And the demon threw him in the midst, and it came out from him, while it did him no harm.
4.36: And great astonishment gripped everyone and they were speaking with one another and they were saying, “What indeed is this message? For with authority and with power he commands the foul spirits, and they come out.”
4.37: And a report about him went out into the whole region that was around them.
4.38: And when Yeshua went out from the synagogue, he entered the house of Shimeon, and Shimeon's mother-in-law was afflicted with a great fever and they besought him for her sake.
4.39: And he stood over her and rebuked her fever and it left her, and at once she arose and she was waiting on them.
4.40: But the sun was setting and they brought to him all of those who were ill with various diseases, and he laid his hand on each one of them and healed them.
4.41: And many demons were going out from many as they screamed and they were saying, “You are The Messiah, the Son of God!” And he was rebuking them and he was not allowing them to say that they knew that he was The Messiah.
4.42: And at daybreak he went out by himself to a deserted place and the crowds were seeking him and came to his place and they held to him that he would not depart from them.
4.43: But Yeshua said to them, “I must proclaim the Kingdom of God to other cities also; it is for this I am sent.”
4.44: And he was preaching in the synagogues of Galilee.

5.1: Now it happened that when the crowd gathered around him to hear the word of God, and he was standing on the side of the Lake of Genesar
5.2: He saw two ships that stood on the shore of the lake and the fishermen who had come down from them and were washing their nets.
5.3: And one of them belonged to Shimeon Kaypha, and Yeshua went up and sat down in it, and he said to take it out a little from the land into the water, and he sat down and he taught the crowds from the ship.
5.4: And when he had ceased speaking, he said to Shimeon, “Take to the deep and cast your nets for a catch.
5.5: Shimeon answered and he said to him, “Rabbi, we worked all night, and we have not caught anything, but at your word, I will cast the net.”
5.6: And when they had done this, they caught very many fish and the net was breaking.
5.7: And they beckoned to their partners who were in another ship to come help them and when they came they filled those two ships, so that they were almost sinking.
5.8: And when Shimeon Kaypha saw it, he fell before the feet of Yeshua and he said to him, “I beg you, my Lord, abandon me, for I am a sinner.”
5.9: For awe had seized him and all of them who were with him, due to that catch of fish which they had caught,
5.10: Likewise also Yaqob and Yohannan, sons of Zebedee, who were partners of Shimeon, but Yeshua said to Shimeon, “Do not be afraid; from now on, you will be catching men for salvation.”
5.11: And they brought those ships to land, and they left everything and they came after him.
5.12: And when Yeshua was in one of the cities, a certain man who was full of leprosy came in and saw Yeshua and he fell down on his face and he begged of him and he said to him, “My Lord, if you are willing, you can purify me.”
5.13: And Yeshua reached his hand and he touched him and he said, “I am willing; be purified.” And immediately his leprosy went from him and he was purified.
5.14: And he commanded him: “Tell no man, but go show yourself to the Priest and offer for your cleansing, just as Moses commanded, for their testimony.”
5.15: And fame went out about him increasingly, and many people were gathered to hear from him and to be healed of their sicknesses.
5.16: But he was departing to the wilderness and praying.
5.17: And it happened on one of the days when Yeshua was teaching, the Pharisees and The Teachers of The Law were sitting who had come from every village of Galilee, and of Judea, and of Jerusalem, and the power of THE LORD JEHOVAH was there to heal them.
5.18: And men brought a man on a pallet who was paralyzed, and they were seeking to enter to place him in front of him.
5.19: And when they found no way to bring him in because of the crowd of people, they went up by themselves to the roof and lowered him with the pallet from the tiles into the center, in front of Yeshua.
5.20: But when Yeshua saw their faith, he said to that paralyzed man: “Man, your sins are forgiven you.”
5.21: And the Scribes and Pharisees began to think, and they were saying, “Who is this who speaks blasphemy? Who is able to forgive sins except God alone?”
5.22: But Yeshua knew their thoughts; he answered and said to them, “What thoughts are entertained by you in your heart?”
5.23: “Which is easier: to say, 'Your sins are forgiven you', or to say, 'Arise and walk?'
5.24: But that you may know that The Son of Man is authorized in the earth to forgive sins”, he said to the paralyzed man, “I say to you, arise, pick up your pallet and go to your house.”
5.25: And at once he arose before their eyes and took up his pallet and he went on to his house as he praised God.
5.26: And astonishment seized everyone and they were praising God, and they were filled with awe and they were saying, “We have seen wonders today.”
5.27: After these things Yeshua went out and he saw a Tax Collector whose name was Levi, who sat in The House of Customs, and he said to him, “Come after me.”
5.28: And he left everything, and rising, he went after him.
5.29: And Levi made a great reception for him in his house and there was a great crowd of Tax Gatherers and of others who were reclining at dinner with them.
5.30: And the Scribes and the Pharisees were complaining and they were saying to his disciples, “Why do you eat and drink with the Tax Collectors and sinners?”
5.31: And Yeshua answered and he said to them, “A doctor is not sought for the healthy but for those who have become very ill.”
5.32: “I have not come that I may call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.”
5.33: But they were saying to him, “Why do the disciples of Yohannan fast faithfully and pray, also the Pharisees, but yours are eating and drinking?”
5.34: But he said to them, “You cannot make the children of the bridal chamber fast as long as the groom is with them.”
5.35: “But the days will come when the groom will be taken from them and then they will fast in those days.”
5.36: And he told them a parable: “No man tears a strip from a new garment and places it on a worn garment, lest also the new tears the worn and it does not resemble the strip from the new piece.”
5.37: “And no man places new wine in old wineskins, lest the new wine should burst the skins and the wine is spilled and the skins are ruined.”
5.38: “But new wine is placed in new wineskins and both are preserved.”
5.39: And no man drinks old wine and at once desires the new, for he says, “The old is sweet.”

6.1: Now it happened on the Sabbath when Yeshua was walking among the grain, his disciples were plucking the ears and were rubbing them in their hands and eating them.
6.2: But some of the Pharisees were saying to them, “Why are you doing what is illegal to do on the Sabbath?”
6.3: Yeshua answered and said to them, “Have you not read this thing that David did when he was hungry and those who were with him,”
6.4: “When he entered the house of God and he took and ate the bread of the table of THE LORD JEHOVAH and he gave to those who were with him, which was not legal to eat except for the Priests only?”
6.5: And he said to them, “The Son of Man is Master of the sabbath.”
6.6: But it happened on another Sabbath that he entered a synagogue and he was teaching, and a man was there whose right hand was shriveled.
6.7: And the Scribes and the Pharisees were watching him whether he would heal on the Sabbath, that they might be able to accuse him.
6.8: But he knew their schemes and he said to that man whose hand was shriveled, “Stand; come to the center of the synagogue”, and when he came and stood,
6.9: Yeshua said to them, “I ask you, what is legal on the Sabbath: to do good or evil? To save life or to destroy?”
6.10: And he gazed at all of them and he said to him, “Straighten your hand”, and he stretched it out and his hand was restored like his other.
6.11: But they were filled with envy, and they were speaking one with another of what they should do to Yeshua.
6.12: But it occurred in those days that Yeshua went out to a mountain to pray, and there he was waiting for the morning in the prayer of God.
6.13: And when it was dawn he called his disciples and he chose twelve of them, whom he named Apostles:
6.14: Shimeon, whom he named Kaypha, and Andraeus his brother, Yaqob and Yohannan and Phillipus and Bar Tolmay
6.15: And Mattay and Thoma and Yaqob Bar Halphai, Shimeon who is called The Zealot,
6.16: And Yehuda Bar Yaqob and Yehuda Scariota who was the traitor.
6.17: And Yeshua descended with them and he stood in the valley and a great crowd of his disciples and a multitude of crowds of people from all of Judea and from Jerusalem and from the sea coast of Tyre and Sidon
6.18: Who came to hear his message and to be healed of their sicknesses, and those who were afflicted by foul spirits, and they were healed.
6.19: And all the crowds were seeking to touch him, for power was proceeding from him and it was healing all of them.
6.20: And he lifted his eyes upon his disciples and he said, “Blessed are you poor ones, because yours is the Kingdom of God.”
6.21: “Blessed are you who hunger now, for you shall be satisfied. Blessed are you who weep now, for you shall laugh.”
6.22: “Blessed are you, whenever men shall hate you and shall separate you and shall insult you and shall cast out your name as evil for The Son of Man's sake.”
6.23: “Rejoice in that day and leap for joy, for your reward is great in Heaven, for so were their fathers doing to The Prophets.”
6.24: “But woe to you rich men, for you have received your comfort!”
6.25: “Woe to you satisfied ones, for you shall hunger!” Woe to you who are laughing now, for you shall weep and you shall wail!”
6.26: “Woe to you when men shall say wonderful things about you, for so were their fathers doing to the false Prophets!” Critical
6.27: “But I say to you who are hearing, Love your enemies and do what is wonderful to those who hate you.”
6.28: “Bless those who curse you, and pray over those who take you away by force.”
6.29: “And to him who strikes you on your cheek, offer the other, and from whoever takes away your cloak, withhold not your coat also.”
6.30: “Give to everyone who asks you and do not demand from him who takes what is yours.”
6.31: “And just as you desire people to do for you, do also for them.”
6.32: “For if you love those who love you, what goodness do you have? Even sinners love those who love them.”
6.33: “And if you are doing good to those who treat you well, what goodness do you have? For even sinners do likewise.”
6.34: “And if you lend to him from whom you expect to be repaid, what goodness do you have? For even sinners lend to sinners to be repaid likewise.”
6.35: “But love your enemies and treat them well and lend and do not cut off the hope of any person, and your reward shall be great and you shall be the children of The Highest because he is kind toward the evil and toward the unbelievers.”
6.36: “Be therefore merciful, just as also your Father is merciful.”
6.37: “Do not judge, and you will not be judged. Do not condemn, and you will not be condemned. Forgive and you will be forgiven.”
6.38: “Give and it will be given to you in good measure, pressed down and overflowing, they shall cast into your lap. For with what measure you measure it will be measured to you.”
6.39: And he told them a parable: “Can a blind man to lead a blind man? Will not both of them fall into a ditch?”
6.40: “There is no disciple greater than his mentor, for everyone who is perfected shall be like his mentor.”
6.41: “But why do you observe a chip in the eye of your brother, but you do not see the plank that is in your eye?”
6.42: “Or how can you say to your brother, 'My brother, let me cast the chip out from your eye, when behold, the plank that is in your own eye is not visible to you? Hypocrite! First cast out the plank from your own eye, and then sight will be given to you to pull out the chip from your brother's eye.”
6.43: “There is not a good tree that produces bad fruit, neither a bad tree that produces good fruit,”
6.44: “For every tree is known by its fruits, for they do not pick figs from thorns, neither do they gather grapes from a bush.”
6.45: “A good man brings forth good from the good treasure that is in his heart and the evil man brings out evil from the evil treasure that is in his heart. For the lips speak from the fullness of the heart.”
6.46: “Why are you calling me, 'My Lord, my Lord'', and you are not doing whatever I say?”
6.47: “Every person who comes to me and has heard my words and does them, I shall show you what he is like”:
6.48: “He is like a man who built a house and he dug and he went deep and laid the foundation on the rock, and when there was a flood, the flood beat on that house and it could not shake it, for its foundation was founded on the rock.”
6.49: “And he who heard and did not do it is like the man who built his house on the soil without a foundation, and when the river beat on it, immediately it fell, and the fall of that house was great.”

7.1: And when he had finished all these words before the audience of the people, Yeshua entered Kapernahum.
7.2: But the servant of a certain Centurion who was precious to him had become ill, and he had come near to death.
7.3: And he had heard about Yeshua, and he sent Elders of the Jews to him and he was begging him to come and save the life of his servant.
7.4: But when they came to Yeshua, they were seeking from him diligently and they were saying, “He is worthy that you should do this for him.”
7.5: “For he loves our nation and also has built a synagogue for us.”
7.6: And Yeshua was going with them and when he was not very far from the house, the Centurion sent his friends and he said to him, “My Lord, you should not trouble yourself, for I am not worthy that you should enter under my roof.”
7.7: “Therefore, I was not worthy to come to you, but say in a word and my boy will be healed.”
7.8: “For I am also a man who is put under authority, and I have soldiers under my hand and I say to this one, 'Go', and he goes, and to another, 'Come', and he comes, and unto my servant, 'Do this', and he does it.”
7.9: But when Yeshua heard these things, he was amazed at him, and he turned and he said to the crowd that came after him, “I say to you that not even in the house of Israel have I found faith like this.”
7.10: And those who had been sent returned to the house and they found that the servant who had been sick was already well.
7.11: And it happened the day after, he went to the city whose name was Nain, and his disciples were with him and the crowds.
7.12: And when he approached the gate of the city, he saw as a dead man was being escorted who had been the only son of his mother, and his mother was a widow, and many of the people of the city had assembled with her.
7.13: But Yeshua saw her and he was moved with pity for her and he said to her, “Do not weep.”
7.14: And he went and touched the palette, and those who were bearing it stood still and he said, “Young man, I say to you, arise.”
7.15: And he who had died sat up and began to speak, and he gave him to his mother.
7.16: And awe gripped all the people, and they were glorifying God, and they were saying, “A great Prophet has arisen among us, and God has visited his people.”
7.17: And this word about him went out in all Judea and in all the region that was around them.
7.18: And his disciples revealed all these things to Yohannan.
7.19: And Yohannan called two of his disciples, and he sent them to Yeshua and he said, “Are you The One who was coming or are we waiting for another?”
7.20: And they came to Yeshua and they were saying to him, “Yohannan The Baptizer has sent us to you, and he said, “Are you the one who was coming or are we waiting for another?”
7.21: But in that hour, he healed many from diseases and from plagues and of evil spirits, and he gave sight to many blind people.
7.22: And Yeshua answered and he said to them, “Go tell Yohannan everything that you have seen and that you have heard; that they who were blind are seeing, and they who were lame are walking, and they who were lepers are cleansed, and they who were deaf are hearing, and they who were dead are raised, and those who were poor are given Good News.”
7.23: “And blessed is he, whoever will not take offense at me.”
7.24: But when the disciples of Yohannan left, he began to say to the crowds about Yohannan, “What did you go out to the wilderness to see? A reed being shaken by the wind?”
7.25: “But what did you go out to see? A man who is clothed in soft garments? Behold, those who are in glorious clothing and in luxury are in a King's house.”
7.26: “But what did you go out to see? A Prophet? Yes, I say to you, and more than a Prophet.”
7.27: “This is he about whom it is written: 'Behold, I am sending my messenger before your face to prepare the way before you.'”
7.28: “I say to you, there is not a Prophet among those born of women who was greater than Yohannan The Baptizer, but a little one in the Kingdom of God is greater than he.”
7.29: And all the people who heard and also the Tax Collectors justified God, for they had been baptized by the baptism of Yohannan.
7.30: But the Pharisees and the Scribes rejected the will of God in their souls, because they were not baptized by him.
7.31: “To what therefore shall I compare the men of this generation, and what does it resemble?”
7.32: “It is like boys sitting in the street and calling their friends and saying, 'We sang to you and you did not dance, and we howled to you and you did not cry.'”
7.33: “For Yohannan The Baptizer came not eating bread neither drinking wine, and you were saying, 'He has a demon.' “
7.34: “The Son of Man came eating and drinking and you were saying, 'Behold, a man a glutton and a drinker of wine and a friend of Tax Collectors and of sinners.' “
7.35: “And wisdom is justified by all its works.”
7.36: But one of the Pharisees came asking him to eat with him and he entered the Pharisee's house and he reclined.
7.37: And a sinner woman who was in the city, when she knew that he was staying in the Pharisee's house, she took an alabaster vase of ointment.
7.38: And she stood behind him at his feet, and she was weeping and she began washing his feet with her tears and wiping them with the hair of her head. And she was kissing his feet and anointing them with ointment.
7.39: But when that Pharisee who had invited him saw, he thought within himself and he said, “If this one were a Prophet, he would have known who she is and what her reputation is, for she is a sinner woman who touched him.”
7.40: But Yeshua answered and he said to him, “Shimeon, I have something to tell you.” But he said to him, “Say it, Rabbi.”
7.41: And Yeshua said to him, “One landowner had two debtors; one debtor owed him 500 denarii and the other 50 denarii.”
7.42: “And because they had nothing to pay he forgave both of them. Which of them therefore will love him more?”
7.43: Shimeon answered and he said, “I suppose that he who was forgiven most.” Yeshua said to him, “You have judged correctly.”
7.44: And he turned to that woman and he said to Shimeon, “Do you see this woman? I entered your house, yet you gave no water for my feet and she has washed my feet with her tears and has wiped them with her hair.”
7.45: “You did not kiss me, but look, from when she entered, she has not ceased to kiss my feet.”
7.46: “You did not anoint my head with oil, but this one has anointed my feet with oil of ointment.”
7.47: “On account of this, I say to you, that her many sins are forgiven her because she loved much, but he who is forgiven a little loves a little.”
7.48: And he said to her, “Woman, your sins are forgiven you.”
7.49: But they who were reclining said in their souls, “Who is this that he even forgives sins?”
7.50: But Yeshua said to that woman, “Your faith has given you life, go in peace.”

8.1: It happened after these things that Yeshua was traveling a circuit in the cities and villages and preaching and announcing the Kingdom of God and his twelve were with him,
8.2: And these women who had been healed from sicknesses and from evil spirits: Maryam who is called Magdalitha, she from whom seven demons had gone out
8.3: And Yohanna the wife of Chuza, the steward of Herodus, and Shushan and many others who were ministering to them of their possessions.
8.4: And when a great crowd had gathered, and they were coming to him from all the cities, he said in a parable:
8.5: “A sower went out to sow his seed, and as he sowed, there was that which fell on the side of the road and it was trampled and a bird ate it.
8.6: And other seed fell on the rock and in an hour it sprang up, and because there was no moisture for it, it dried up.
8.7: And other seed fell among thorns and the thorns sprang up with it and they choked it.
8.8: Other seed fell in good and excellent ground, and it sprang up and produced fruit one hundred fold. And when he had said these things, he cried, “He that has an ear to hear, let him hear.”
8.9: And his disciples asked him, “What is this parable?”
8.10: But he said to them, “It has been given to you to know the secret of the Kingdom of God, but to those others, it is spoken in an allegory, that while seeing they will not perceive, and when hearing, they will not understand.”
8.11: “But this is the parable: the seed is the word of God.”
8.12: “But those upon the side of the road are they who hear the word, and the enemy comes and takes the word from their heart, lest they should believe and they should live.”
8.13: “But these upon the rock are they, who when they have heard, they receive the word with joy, and there is no root in them, but their faith is temporary and in time of temptation they are subverted.”
8.14: “But these which fell among thorns are those who hear the word and by cares and by riches and the desires of the world, they are choked and they yield no fruit.”
8.15: “But these which are on the good ground are those who hear the word with a pure and good heart, and they hold onto it and they yield fruit with patience.”
8.16: “No man lights a lamp and covers it with a vessel or sets it under a bed, but he sets it upon a lamp stand that everyone who enters may see its light.”
8.17: “For there is nothing covered that will not be revealed, neither hidden that will not be made known and come into the open.”
8.18: “Take heed how you hear. Whoever has it, it will be given to him, and whoever does not have it, also that which he thinks he has will be taken from him.”
8.19: But his mother and his brothers came to him, and they were not able to speak with him, because of the crowd.
8.20: They said to him, “Your mother and your brothers are standing outside and they desire to see you.”
8.21: But he answered and said to them, “These are my mother and my brothers; those who are hearing the word of God and are practicing it.”
8.22: But it was on one of those days, Yeshua went up and sat down in the boat, he and his disciples, and he told them, “Let us cross to the other side of the lake.”
8.23: And as he journeyed, Yeshua himself was asleep and there was a tempest of wind on the lake and the ship was coming close to sinking.
8.24: And they came and they awakened him and they were saying to him, “Our Master, our Master, we are being destroyed!” But he arose and rebuked the wind and the waves of the seas, and they ceased and there was calm.
8.25: And he said to them, “Where is your faith?” But they, being in awe, were marveling, and they were saying one to another, “Who is this, truly, who commands even the wind, the waves and the sea, and they obey him?”
8.26: And they sailed and they came to the region of the Gadarenes which is on the other side next to Galilee.
8.27: And when he unboarded to land, a man met him from the city who had a demon in him for a long time. He was not wearing clothes, and he was not living in a house but among the tombs.
8.28: And when he saw Yeshua, he screamed and he fell before him, and with a loud voice, he said, “What business do we have with you Yeshua, the Son of God Most High? I beg of you, do not punish me!”
8.29: For Yeshua had commanded the vile spirit to go out from the man, as it had possessed him for a long time. And he had been kept bound in chains and in shackles and he would burst his bonds and he would be driven by the demon into the desert.
8.30: But Yeshua asked him, “What is your name?” But he said to him, “Legion”, because many demons had entered him.
8.31: And they were begging him that he would not command them to enter the abyss.
8.32: Now a herd of many swine was there grazing on the mountain, and they were begging him to permit them to enter the swine, and he permitted them.
8.33: And the demons went out from the man and they entered the swine and the whole herd went straight to the precipice and they fell into the lake and drowned.
8.34: But when the herdsmen saw what happened, they fled, and they related it in the city and in the villages.
8.35: And people went out to see the thing that happened and they came to Yeshua, and they found that man, whose demons had gone out, being clothed, sober and sitting at the feet of Yeshua, and they were in awe.
8.36: And those who had seen it related to them how the demoniac man had been healed.
8.37: And the crowds of Gadarenes were all begging him that he would go from them, because great fear had seized them, and he, Yeshua, embarked the ship and returned from among them.
8.38: But that man from whom the demons had gone out was begging him that he might join him and Yeshua dismissed him and he said to him:
8.39: “Return to your house and relate the thing that God has done for you.” And he went on, and he was preaching in the whole city the thing that Yeshua had done for him.
8.40: But when Yeshua returned, a great crowd received him, for they had all been looking for him.
8.41: And one man whose name was Yorash, head of the synagogue, fell before the feet of Yeshua and he begged him to enter his house.
8.42: For he had an only daughter, about twelve years old, and she was about to die, and when Yeshua went with him a great crowd was pressing him.
8.43: Now there was certain woman whose blood had flowed twelve years, who spent all her property among physicians, and she could not be healed by anyone.
8.44: She approached him from behind, and she touched the fringe of his garment and immediately the flow of her blood stopped.
8.45: And Yeshua said, “Who touched me?” And when everyone denied, Shimeon Kaypha and those with him said to him, “Our master, the crowds are pressing close to you, and they are pushing and you say, 'Who touched me?' “
8.46: But he said, “Someone has touched me, for I know that power has gone out from me.”
8.47: When the woman saw that she had not escaped his notice, she came trembling, and she fell and she worshiped him, and she told before the eyes of all the people for what cause she had touched him, and how she had been healed immediately.
8.48: But Yeshua said to her, “Take heart, my daughter; your faith has given you life; go in peace.”
8.49: And while he was speaking, the man who was of the house of the leader of the synagogue came and he said to him, “Your daughter has died; do not trouble the teacher.”
8.50: But Yeshua heard and he said to the father of the girl, “Do not be afraid; only have faith, and she will live.”
8.51: But Yeshua came to the house and he did not allow anyone to enter with him except Shimeon, Yaqob, Yohannan, the father of the girl and her mother.
8.52: And all of them were weeping and wailing over her but Yeshua said, “Stop weeping; for she is not dead, but she is sleeping.”
8.53: And they were laughing at him, for they knew that she had died.
8.54: But he sent everyone outside and he held her by her hand and he called her, and he said, “Little girl, arise.”
8.55: And her spirit returned, and at once she arose, and he commanded them to give her food.
8.56: And her parents were staggered, but he warned them not to tell anyone what had happened.

9.1: And Yeshua called the twelve and he gave power to them and authority over all demons and diseases to heal the sick.
9.2: And he sent them to preach the Kingdom of God and to heal the sick.
9.3: And he said to them, “You shall take nothing for the road; no staff, neither moneybag, nor bread, nor money, neither two tunics will be with you.”
9.4: “And whatever house you enter, stay there and from there go out.”
9.5: “And whoever does not receive you, whenever you leave that city, shake off even the sand of your feet against them for a testimony.”
9.6: And The Apostles went out and were walking around in the villages and cities and they were preaching The Good News and healing in every place.
9.7: But Herodus the Tetrarch heard all the things that were being done by his hand and he was amazed because the people were saying, “Yohannan has arisen from among the dead.”
9.8: But others were saying, “Elijah has appeared”, and others, “A Prophet among the ancient Prophets has risen.”
9.9: And Herodus said, “I have cut off the head of Yohannan, but who is this about whom I have heard these things?” And he wanted to see him.
9.10: And when The Apostles returned, they were relating to Yeshua everything that they had done and he took them by themselves to a deserted region of Bethsaida.
9.11: But when the crowds knew, they went after him and he received them and he was speaking with them about the Kingdom of God, and those who were in need of healing he healed.
9.12: But when the day began to decline, his disciples came and they were saying to him, “Dismiss the crowds that they go to the surrounding villages and hamlets to lodge in them and to find provisions for themselves, because we are in a deserted place.”
9.13: Yeshua said to them, “You give them food.” But they were saying, “We don't have more than five loaves and two fish, unless we go and buy provisions for this entire people.”
9.14: For there were about five thousand men, and Yeshua said, “Make them recline by groups of fifty men in a group.”
9.15: And the disciples did so and they made them all recline.
9.16: And Yeshua took those five loaves and two fish, and he gazed into Heaven and he blessed and he broke and he gave to his disciples to set before the crowds.
9.17: And they all ate and were satisfied, and they took up the fragments that remained, twelve large baskets.
9.18: And when he was praying alone, and his disciples were with him, he asked them and he said, “What is it the crowds are saying about me, who I am?”
9.19: They answered and they were saying to him, “Yohannan the Baptizer”, and others, “Elijah”, and others, “A Prophet, one of the ancient Prophets has risen.”
9.20: But he said to them, “Who is it you are saying that I am?” Shimeon answered and he said, “The Messiah of God.”
9.21: But he admonished them and he warned them that they should not say this to anyone.
9.22: And he said to them, “The Son of Man is going to suffer many things and to be rejected by the Elders and the Chief Priests and Scribes, and they will murder him, and on the third day he will arise.”
9.23: And he said before everyone, “Whoever is willing to come after me, let him deny himself, let him take up his cross everyday, and let him come after me.”
9.24: “For whoever wills to save his soul, destroys it; but whoever will give up his soul for my sake, this one saves it.”
9.25: “For how would a man benefit to gain the whole world but destroy his soul or lose it?”
9.26: “Whoever will be ashamed of me and of my words, of that one will The Son of Man be ashamed whenever he comes in the glory of his Father with his Holy Angels.”
9.27: “I tell you the truth; there are men who stand here who will not partake of death until they behold the Kingdom of God.”
9.28: But it happened after these words, about eight days later, Yeshua took Shimeon and Yaqob and Yohannan and he went up a mountain to pray.
9.29: And as he prayed, the appearance of his face was transfigured and his garments became white and they were shining.
9.30: And behold, two men were speaking with him, who are Moses and Elijah,
9.31: Who appeared in glory, but they were speaking about his exodus which was going to be fulfilled in Jerusalem.
9.32: And Shimeon and those with him were groggy with sleep, and they awakened with difficulty, and they saw his glory and those two men who were standing with him.
9.33: And when they began to part from him, Shimeon said to Yeshua, “Rabbi, it is beautiful for us to be here. Let us make three Tabernacles: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.” And he did not know what he said.
9.34: And as he said these things, there was a cloud that formed a tabernacle about them and they were afraid when they beheld Moses and Elijah who entered into the cloud.
9.35: And there was a voice from the cloud that said, “This is my Son, The Beloved; Hear him.”
9.36: And when the voice had occurred, Yeshua was found alone, and they were silent and told no man in those days what they had seen.
9.37: And it occurred the day after as they descended from the mountain, a great crowd met them.
9.38: And one man from that crowd called and he said, “Teacher, I beg you, restore my son to me; he is the only child I have.”
9.39: “And a spirit suddenly comes upon him, and suddenly he screams and gnashes his teeth and becomes ill, and it departs from him with difficulty whenever it attacks him.”
9.40: “And I begged of your disciples to cast it out and they could not.”
9.41: Then Yeshua answered and said, “Oh generation without faith and perverse! How long shall I be with you and endure you? Bring your son here.”
9.42: And when he came near to him that demon threw him down and convulsed him. And Yeshua rebuked the foul spirit and he healed the boy and he gave him to his father.
9.43: And they were all astonished at the majesty of God, and as everyone was marveling at everything that Yeshua did, he said to his disciples:
9.44: “Put these words in your ears, for The Son of Man is going to be delivered into the hands of men.”
9.45: They did not understand the saying because it was hidden from them lest they should perceive it, and they were afraid to ask him about this saying.
9.46: And deliberation entered among them of who was great among them.
9.47: But Yeshua knew the thought of their heart, and he took a boy and he stood him by him.
9.48: And he said to them, “Whoever receives a boy like this one in my name, that one receives me, and whoever receives me receives the one who sent me, for whoever is least among you all, this one will be great.”
9.49: And Yohannan answered and said, “Our master, we saw a man who cast out a demon in your name and we forbade him, for he does not come after you with us.”
9.50: Yeshua said to them, “You shall not forbid, for whoever is not against you is for you.”
9.51: And it was that when the days of his ascent were fulfilled, he prepared himself to go to Jerusalem.
9.52: And he sent messengers before his presence and they went and entered a village of the Samaritans so as to prepare for him.
9.53: And they did not receive him because his person was determined to go to Jerusalem.
9.54: And when Yaqob and Yohannan his disciples saw it, they were saying to him, “Our Lord, do you want us to speak and fire will descend from Heaven, and will consume them as Elijah also did?”
9.55: And he turned and he rebuked them and he said, “You do not know of which Spirit you are.”
9.56: “For The Son of Man has not come to destroy lives but to give life.” And they went to other villages.
9.57: And as they were going on the road, a man said to him, “I shall come after you to the place that you will go, my lord.”
9.58: Yeshua said to him, “Foxes have dens and birds of the sky have shelters, but The Son of Man does not have a place to lay his head.”
9.59: And he said to another, “Come after me.” But he said to him, “My lord, permit me first to go bury my Father.”
9.60: And Yeshua said to him, “Let the dead bury their dead and you go announce the Kingdom of God.”
9.61: And another said to him, “I shall come after you, my lord, but permit me first to say goodbye to my children, and I shall come.”
9.62: Yeshua said to him, “No man lays his hands to plow and gazes behind him and is fit for the Kingdom of God.”

10.1: After these things Yeshua appointed another seventy of his disciples and he sent them two by two before his presence to every place and city where he was prepared to go.
10.2: And he said to them, “The harvest is great and the workers are few; pray therefore the Lord of the harvest to send workers to his harvest.”
10.3: “Go; behold, I am sending you as sheep among wolves.”
10.4: “You shall not take for yourselves moneybags, nor wallets, nor sandals and do not greet a man on the road.”
10.5: “And to whatever house you enter, first say, 'Peace to this house.' “
10.6: “And if a son of peace is there, your peace shall rest upon it, but if not, it will return to you.”
10.7: “But stay in that house while you are eating and drinking of what is theirs, for the worker is worthy of his fare, and do not move from house to house.”
10.8: “And whatever city you enter, and they receive you, eat anything that is offered to you.”
10.9: And heal those who are sick in it and say to them, “The Kingdom of God has come near to you.”
10.10: “But whichever city you enter and they will not receive you, go out to the street and say”:
10.11: 'Even the sand that cleaves to our feet from your city we wipe off unto you, yet know this, The Kingdom of God has come near to you.'
10.12: “I say to you that it shall be pleasant for Sadom in that day, compared to that city.”
10.13: “Woe to you Korazin, woe to you Bethsaida, because if the miracles had occurred in Tyre and Tsidon that have occurred in you, they doubtless would have repented in sackcloth and in ashes.
10.14: “Yet for Tyre and for Tsidon it shall be pleasant in the day of judgment compared to you.”
10.15: “And you Kapernahum, she that was exalted unto Heaven, you shall be debased unto Sheol.”
10.16: “Whoever listens to you listens to me, and whoever rejects you, rejects me, and whoever rejects me rejects The One who sent me.”
10.17: And those seventy whom he had sent returned with great joy and they were saying to him, “Our Lord, even the demons are subject to us in your name.”
10.18: But he said to them, “I was beholding Satan himself who fell like lightning from Heaven.”
10.19: “Behold, I have given you authority that you may tread on snakes and scorpions and all the power of the enemy and nothing will harm you.”
10.20: “However, you should not rejoice in this, that the demons are subject to you, but rejoice that your names are written in Heaven.”
10.21: And in that hour Yeshua triumphed in The Spirit of Holiness and he said, “I thank you, my Father, Lord of Heaven and earth, that you have hidden these things from the wise and learned and have revealed them to infants; yes, my Father, for thus it was a pleasure before you.”
10.22: And he turned to his disciples and he said to them, “Everything has been delivered to me from my Father, and no man knows who The Son is except The Father only, and who The Father is except The Son, and he to whom The Son will be pleased to reveal him.”
10.23: And he turned to his disciples by themselves and he said, “Blessed are those eyes that are seeing whatever you are seeing.”
10.24: “For I say to you that many Prophets and Kings have desired to see the things that you are seeing, and they have not seen, and to hear the things that you are hearing and they have not heard.”
10.25: And behold, a scribe arose to test him, and he said, “Teacher, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?”
10.26: But Yeshua said to him, “How is it written in the law? How do you read it?”
10.27: He answered and he said to him, “You shall love THE LORD JEHOVAH your God from all your heart and from all your soul and from all your strength and from all your mind and your neighbor as yourself.”
10.28: Yeshua said to him, “You have said correctly; do this and you shall live.”
10.29: But as he wanted to justify himself, he said to him, “And who is my neighbor?”
10.30: Yeshua said to him, “A certain man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho and robbers fell upon him, and they plundered and beat him and left him when little life remained in him and they departed.”
10.31: “And it happened a certain priest was going down that road and he saw him and passed by.”
10.32: “And so also a Levite coming arrived at that place and he saw him and he passed by.”
10.33: “But a Samaritan man as he traveled came where he was and he saw him and he took pity on him.”
10.34: “And he came and bound his wounds and poured wine and oil on them and set him on his donkey and he took him to an inn and cared for him.”
10.35: “And at the break of day, he produced two denarii and gave them to the innkeeper and he said to him, 'Take care of him and if you spend anything more, whenever I return I will give it to you.' “
10.36: “Who therefore of these three appears to you to have been a neighbor to him who fell into the hands of the robbers?”
10.37: But he said, “He who took pity on him.” Yeshua said to him, “You go and do likewise.”
10.38: And it was that when they were traveling on a road, he entered a certain village and a woman whose name was Martha received him into her house.
10.39: And she had a sister, whose name was Maryam, and she came and sat herself at the feet of Our Lord, and she was listening to his words.
10.40: But Martha was busy with serving many things and she came and said to him, “My lord, does it not concern you that my sister has left me alone to serve? Tell her to help me.”
10.41: But Yeshua answered and said to her, “Martha, Martha, you take pains and are troubled about many things.”
10.42: “But one thing is necessary; Maryam has chosen that good part for herself which will not be taken away from her.”

11.1: It was that when he was praying in a certain place, when he finished, one of his disciples said to him, “Our Lord, teach us to pray, just as Yohannan also taught his disciples.”
11.2: Yeshua said to them, “When you pray, you shall say thus: 'Our Father, who are in Heaven'
11.3: 'Hallowed be your name, let your Kingdom come, let your will be done also in the earth as it is in Heaven. Give us our necessary bread every day,'
11.4: 'And forgive us our sins even as we forgive all who are indebted to us, and do not lead us into temptation, but save us from The Evil One.'”
11.5: And he said to them, “Who among you has a friend and will go to him at midnight and will say to him, “My friend, lend me three loaves”,
11.6: “For my friend has come to me from the road and I have nothing that I can set before him,”
11.7: “And his friend from within will answer and will say to him, 'Do not disturb me, for behold, the door is bolted and my children are in bed with me; I cannot rise and give to you?' “
11.8: “I say to you that if because of friendship he will not give to him, because of his persistence he will arise and he will give him as much as he needs.”
11.9: “I am also saying to you, Ask and it shall be given to you, seek and you shall find, knock and it will be opened to you.”
11.10: “For everyone who asks receives, and whoever seeks finds, and whoever knocks, it is opened to him.”
11.11: Which of you is a father whose son will ask him for bread and would hand him a stone, and if he asks him for a fish will, instead of a fish, hand him a snake,”
11.12: “And if he would ask of him an egg, would give him a scorpion?”
11.13: “And if you, who are evil, know to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father in Heaven give The Spirit of Holiness to those who ask him?”
11.14: And when he was casting out a demon that was making a person mute, it happened that when that demon went out, the mute spoke, and the crowds were astonished.
11.15: But men among them said, “This one exorcises devils by Beelzebub the Chief of devils.”
11.16: But others were testing him, and they were asking him for a sign from Heaven.
11.17: But because Yeshua knew their thoughts he said to them, “Every realm that is divided against itself will be laid waste, and the home that is divided against its nature falls.”
11.18: And if Satan is divided against himself, how will his Kingdom stand?; for you say that by Beelzebub I cast out devils.”
11.19: “And if I am exorcising devils by Beelzebub, by whom are your children exorcising? Because of this they will be your judges.”
11.20: “But if I cast out devils by the finger of God, the Kingdom of God has come near to you.”
11.21: “Whenever an armed strongman keeps his homestead, his property is at peace.”
11.22: “But if one who is stronger than he will come to overpower him, he takes all his weapons in which he was trusting and he divides his spoil.”
11.23: “Whoever is not with me is against me and whoever does not gather with me is scattering.”
11.24: “Whenever a vile spirit has gone out from a man, it goes traveling around in places where there is no water to seek rest for itself; Whenever it does not find it, it says, 'I shall return to my house from whence I came.' “
11.25: “And when it has come, it finds it swept and decorated.”
11.26: “And it goes bringing seven others spirits which are more evil than itself, and they are entering and dwelling there, and the end of that man will be worse than his beginning.”
11.27: And while he was speaking these things, a woman raised her voice from the crowd and she said to him, “Blessing to the womb that carried you and to the breasts that suckled you.”
11.28: He said to her, “Blessings to those who have heard the word of God and keep it.”
11.29: And when the crowds were assembling he began to say, “This wicked generation seeks for a sign and a sign will not be given to it except the sign of Jonah the Prophet.”
11.30: “For just as Jonah was a sign to the Ninevites thus also The Son of Man shall be to this generation.”
11.31: The Queen of The South shall stand in judgment with the people of this generation, and she shall condemn them, for she came from the far side of the land that she might hear the wisdom of Solomon and behold, he who is greater than Solomon is here.”
11.32: “The Ninevite men will rise in judgment with this generation and they shall condemn it because they repented at the preaching of Jonah and behold, he who is greater than Jonah is here.”
11.33: “No man lights a lamp and sets it in a hidden place or under a bushel but over a lamp stand, that those who enter may see its light.”
11.34: “The lamp of the body is your eye; when therefore your eye is clear your whole body shall be illuminated, but if it should be bad, your body also shall be darkened.”
11.35: “Take care therefore lest the light that is in you is darkness”
11.36: “But if your whole body is enlightened, and there is no darkened part, it shall be shining entirely like a lamp giving you light by its flame”
11.37: “But while he was speaking, one Pharisee requested of him that he would dine with him, and he entered and reclined.”
11.38: But that Pharisee when he saw him, he was amazed that he did not wash before his dinner.
11.39: But Yeshua said to him, “Now you Pharisees are cleansing the outside of the cup and of the dish, but the inside of some of you is full of rape and wickedness.”
11.40: “Mindless ones! Has not The One who made the outside also made the inside?”
11.41: “However, give whatever you have in charity and behold, everything is pure to you.”
11.42: “But woe to you Pharisees, for you tithe mint and dill and every herb and you pass over justice and over the love of God, but these it was necessary for you to do and not that you should forsake the other!”
11.43: “Woe to you Pharisees who love first class seats in the synagogues and greetings in the streets!”
11.44: “Woe to you, Scribes and Pharisees, phonies, who are like tombs that are not known, and the children of men walk over them and do not know.”
11.45: And one of the Scribes answered and he said to him, “Teacher, when you say these things, you insult us also.”
11.46: But he said, “Woe to you also, you Scribes, for you load the children of men with heavy burdens and you will not touch those burdens with one of your fingers.”
11.47: “Woe to you who are building the tombs of The Prophets, for your fathers murdered them.”
11.48: “You testify therefore and you approve the deeds of your fathers, for they murdered them and you build their tombs.”
11.49: “Because of this The Wisdom of God said, “Behold, I am sending Prophets and Apostles to them, and some of them they shall persecute and murder”,
11.50: “So that the blood of all The Prophets that was shed from when the world was created shall be required of this generation,”
11.51: “From the blood of Abel and unto the blood of Zachariah who was killed between The Temple and the altar; Yes, I say to you, that it shall be required of this generation.”
11.52: “Woe to you Scribes, because you have taken away the keys of knowledge! You have not entered and those who are entering you have hindered.”
11.53: And when he had said these things to them, the Scribes and Pharisees began to be offended at them and they were angered and they disparaged his words.
11.54: And they acted deceitfully toward him in many things, while they sought to seize on something from his mouth so that they would be able to accuse him.

12.1: And when multitudes of great crowds were gathered together so that they would tread on one another, Yeshua began to say to his disciples, “First, beware among yourselves of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is phoniness.”
12.2: “For there is nothing covered that will not be revealed, neither anything secret that will not be known.”
12.3: “For everything that you will say in darkness shall be heard in the light and whatever you whispered in an ear in an inner chamber will be preached on the rooftops.”
12.4: “And I say to you my friends, do not be afraid of those who kill the body and after this there is nothing more for them to do.”
12.5: “But I will show you whom you should fear: that one who after he kills is authorized to cast into Gehenna; yes I say to you, be afraid of this one.”
12.6: “Are not five sparrows sold for two assarii, and not one of them is forgotten before God?”
12.7: “But every hair of the hairs of your heads is numbered, therefore you shall not be afraid, because you are better than a multitude of sparrows.”
12.8: “And I say to you that everyone who will confess me before the children of men, The Son of Man will also confess him before the Angels of God.”
12.9: “But whoever denies me before the children of men shall himself be denied before the Angels of God.”
12.10: “And everyone who will say a word against The Son of Man shall be forgiven, but whoever blasphemes The Spirit of Holiness, it will not be forgiven to him.”
12.11: “But whenever they bring you to assemblies before rulers and authorities, you shall not be concerned how you will give an answer or what you will say.”
12.12: “For The Spirit of Holiness shall teach you in that hour what it is necessary for you to say.”
12.13: A man from that crowd said to him, “Teacher, tell my brother to divide the inheritance with me.”
12.14: But Yeshua said to him, “Man, who has set me as a judge and a divider over you?”
12.15: And he said to his disciples, “Beware of all greed, because life is not in the abundance of riches.”
12.16: And he told a parable to them: “A certain rich man's land brought him many crops.”
12.17: “And he thought to himself and said, 'What shall I do, for there is no place for me to gather my crops?' “
12.18: “And he said, 'I shall do this: I shall pull down my barns, and I shall build and enlarge them, and I shall gather all my produce and my goods.' “
12.19: “And I shall say to my soul, 'My soul, you have many goods laid up for many years, be contented, eat, drink and be merry.' “
12.20: “Then God said to him, 'Fool, in this night your soul will be required from you, and whose will these things be which you have prepared?' “
12.21: “Thus is whoever lays down a treasure for himself and is not rich in God.”
12.22: And he said to his disciples, “Because of this, I say to you that you shall not be taking pains for yourselves what you will eat, neither for your bodies, what you will wear.”
12.23: “For the soul is more important than food, and the body than clothing.”
12.24: “Consider the ravens that they do not sow, neither do they reap; there is no inner chamber or granary for them, and God feeds them; therefore, how much more important are you than the birds?”
12.25: “Which of you by taking pains can add one cubit to his height?”
12.26: “But if you cannot do the small thing, why do you take pains over the rest?”
12.27: “Consider the lilies how they grow, that they do not labor neither do they weave, but I say to you that not even Solomon in all his glory was robed like one of these.”
12.28: “But if God so clothes the grass that today is in the field and tomorrow will fall into a fire, how much better is he to you, O' small of faith?”
12.29: “You should not seek what you will eat and what you will drink, neither should your mind be distracted by these things.”
12.30: “For all the people of the world are seeking these things, for your Father knows that these things are necessary for you.”
12.31: “However, seek The Kingdom of God, and all these things are added to you.”
12.32: “Fear not, little flock, for your Father desires to give you The Kingdom.”
12.33: “Sell your possessions and give charity; make for yourselves pouches that do not wear out and treasure that is not diminished in Heaven, where a thief does not come in and a moth does not devour.”
12.34: “For wherever your treasure is, there shall your heart be also.”
12.35: “Let your waist be girded and your lamps burning.”
12.36: “Be like people waiting for their Lord when he will return from the wedding chamber, so that whenever he comes and knocks, they may open to him at once.”
12.37: “Blessings to those servants whom, when their Lord will come, he shall find while they are watching. Amen, I say to you, that he shall gird his waist and he shall make them recline and he shall go through and shall wait on them.”
12.38: “And if he will come in the second or third watch, and he will find them thus, blessings to those servants.”
12.39: “Know this, that if the owner of a house had known in which watch a thief would come, he would have been awakened and would not have allowed his house to be broken into.”
12.40: “Be you also ready therefore, for in that hour when you are not expecting, The Son of Man comes.”
12.41: Shimeon Kaypha said to him, “Our Lord, are you saying this parable to us or to everyone also?”
12.42: Yeshua said to him, “Who is the truly faithful and wise steward, whom his Lord will appoint over his servants to give a portion in his time?”
12.43: “Blessed is that servant whom, when his Lord will come, he will find doing so.”
12.44: “Truly, I say to you, that he will appoint him over all his possessions.”
