12.45: “But if that servant will say in his heart, 'My Lord delays to come', and he should begin to strike the servants and the maids of his Lord and he should begin to eat and to drink and to get drunk.”
12.46: “The Lord of that servant will come in a day when he does not expect and in an hour when he does not know; he shall cut him off and he shall appoint his portion with those who are unbelievers.”
12.47: “The servant who knew the will of his master and did not prepare for him according to his will, he shall be beaten many times.”
12.48: But he who did not know and did something worthy of blows shall be beaten with few blows, for from everyone to whom much is given, much shall be required, and of him to whom they commit much, they shall require more.”
12.49: “I have come to set fire to the earth, and I only wish it were already burning.”
12.50: “I have a baptism with which to be baptized, and I am much afflicted until it is fulfilled.”
12.51: “Do you think that I have come to bring peace to the earth? I say to you, no, but division.”
12.52: “From now on there shall be five in one house who are divided, three against two and two against three.”
12.53: “For a father will be divided against his son and son against his father, mother against her daughter and daughter against her mother, mother-in-law against her daughter-in-law and daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.”
12.54: He said to the crowds, “Whenever you see a cloud that rises from the West, and at once you say, 'Rain is coming', it is so.”
12.55: “And when a south wind blows, you say, 'It will be hot', and it is.”
12.56: “Accepters of faces, you know to discern the face of the earth and sky, how do you not discern this time?”
12.57: “Why do you not judge the truth from yourselves?”
12.58: “For whenever you go with your plaintiff to the Ruler, while you are on the road, give merchandise and be ransomed from him, lest he bring you to the Judge and the Judge deliver you to the Officer and the Officer should cast you into prison.”
12.59: “I say to you, you shall not go out from there until you give the last quarter cent.”

13.1: At that time some people came and told him about those Galileans whose blood Pilate had mingled with their sacrifices.
13.2: And Yeshua answered and said to them, “Do you think that these Galileans were sinners more so than all Galileans because this happened to them?”
13.3: “No, but I say to you, all of you also shall likewise be destroyed unless you repent.”
13.4: “Or those eighteen upon whom the tower of Shilokha fell and killed them; do you think that they were sinners more so than all the children of men who dwell in Jerusalem?”
13.5: “No, but I say to you, unless you repent, all of you shall be destroyed like they were.”
13.6: And he told this parable: “A man had a fig tree that was planted in his vineyard and he came seeking fruit on it and he found none.
13.7: And he said to the cultivator, 'Behold, three years I have come seeking fruit in this fig tree, and I find none; cut it down; why is it taking up space?'
13.8: The cultivator said to him, 'My Lord, leave it also this year, while I shall cultivate it and I shall manure it',
13.9: And perhaps it will have borne fruit, otherwise next year you should cut it down.”
13.10: But when Yeshua taught on the Sabbath in one of the synagogues,
13.11: A woman was there who had a spirit of affliction eighteen years, and she was bent over, and she had not been able to be straightened at all.
13.12: But Yeshua saw her and he called her and said to her, “Woman, you are released from your affliction.”
13.13: And he laid his hand upon her and at once she was straightened, and she glorified God.
13.14: But the Leader of the synagogue, being angered, answered, because Yeshua had healed on the Sabbath, and he said to the gathering, “There are six days in which it is legal to work; you may come in them and be healed and not on the Sabbath day.”
13.15: But Yeshua answered and he said to him, “Hypocrite! Does not each one of you on the Sabbath release his ox or his donkey from the stall and go to water it?”
13.16: “But this is a daughter of Abraham and The Devil has bound her, behold, for eighteen years. Is it illegal that she be released from this bondage on the Sabbath Day?”
13.17: And when he had said these things, those who had opposed him were all ashamed, and all the people were rejoicing at all the miracles that were wrought by his hand.
13.18: But Yeshua said, “What is the Kingdom of God like and to what shall I compare it?”
13.19: “It is like a grain of mustard seed, which a man took and cast into his garden, and it grew and became a great tree and a bird of the sky nested in its branches.”
13.20: Again, Yeshua said, “To what shall I compare the Kingdom of God?”
13.21: “It is like leaven which a woman took and hid in three 3-gallon measures of flour until all of it was fermented.”
13.22: And he traveled in villages and cities as he taught and he went on to Jerusalem.
13.23: But a man asked him if there are few who have life.
13.24: But Yeshua said to them, “Strive hard to enter the narrow gate, for I say to you, many shall seek to enter and they shall not be able.”
13.25: “Then the Lord of the house will rise and shall bolt the gate, and you will be standing outside knocking at the gate and you will begin to say, 'Our Lord, Our Lord, open to us.” And he shall answer and he shall say, 'I say to you, that I do not know you. From where are you?' “
13.26: “And you shall begin to say, 'We ate and we drank before you and you taught in our streets.' “
13.27: “And he shall say to you, 'I do not know you and from where you are; depart from me, workers of lies.' “
13.28: “There will be weeping and gnashing teeth when you will behold Abraham and Isaaq and Jaqob and all The Prophets in the Kingdom of God, but you shall be cast out.”
13.29: “And they shall come from The East and from The West and from The South and from The North and they shall recline in the Kingdom of God.”
13.30: “And behold, there are the last who shall be the first and there are the first who shall be the last.”
13.31: That day, some of the Pharisees approached, and they were saying to him, “Depart from here, because Herodus wants to kill you.”
13.32: But Yeshua said to them, “Go tell this Fox, 'Behold, I cast out demons and do cures today and tomorrow and on the third day I shall be perfected.' “
13.33: “However, it is right for me that today and tomorrow I should work and I should go the following day, because it is not possible that a Prophet shall perish outside of Jerusalem.”
13.34: “Jerusalem, Jerusalem, you have murdered The Prophets and you have stoned those who were sent to her. How many times I have desired to gather your children as a hen that gathers her chicks under her wings, and you were not willing!”
13.35: “Behold, your house is left to you desolate, for I say to you, you shall not see me until you will say, 'Blest is he who comes in the name of THE LORD JEHOVAH!'”

14.1: And it was that when he entered the house of one of the leaders of the Pharisees to eat bread on the Sabbath day, and they were observing him,
14.2: Behold, one man who was swollen with fluid was there before him.
14.3: And Yeshua answered and said to the Scribes and to the Pharisees, “Surely it is legal to heal on the Sabbath.”
14.4: But they were silent, and he held him and healed him and he dismissed him.
14.5: And he said to them, “Who of you, whose son or ox should fall in a pit on the Sabbath day, would not at once pull and lift him out?”
14.6: And they could not give him an answer to this.
14.7: And he told a parable to those who were invited there when he saw those who chose the places of the best seats.
14.8: “When you are invited by a man to a banquet house, do not go seat yourself at the best seat, lest it may be that he will appoint a man there who is more honorable than you.”
14.9: “And he who invited you and him should come and say to you, 'Give the place to this man', and you will be ashamed when you stand and you take the last place.”
14.10: “But whenever you are invited, seat yourself last, that whenever he who invited you should come, he may say to you, 'My friend, come up and be seated', and there shall be glory to you before all of those who sit with you.”
14.11: “Because everyone who will exalt himself shall be humbled and everyone who will humble himself shall be exalted.”
14.12: Then he said also to the one who had invited him, “Whenever you make a banquet or supper, do not call your friends, neither your brothers, nor your relatives, nor your rich neighbors, lest also they should invite you and this would be a reward to you.”
14.13: “But whenever you make a reception, invite the poor, the disabled, the maimed and the blind.”
14.14: “And you will be blessed, for they have nothing to repay you, for your reward will be in the resurrection of the righteous.”
14.15: But when one of those who were sitting there heard these things, he said to him, “Blessing to him who will eat bread in the Kingdom of God.”
14.16: Yeshua said to him, “A certain man made a great supper and he called many.”
14.17: “He sent his servant at the time of the supper to say to those who were invited, 'Behold, everything is ready for you; Come.' “
14.18: “And they began each and every one of them to make excuses; the first said to him, 'I have bought a field, and I am compelled to go to see it. I beg of you, allow me to be excused.' “
14.19: “Another said, 'I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I am going to examine them; I beg of you, allow me to be excused.'”
14.20: “Another said, 'I have taken a wife, and therefore I cannot come.' “
14.21: “And that servant came and he told his employer these things; and the owner of the house was angry and he said to his servant, 'Go out quickly to the marketplaces and to the streets of the city and bring here the poor and the afflicted and the feeble and the blind.' “
14.22: “And the servant said, 'My lord, it is as you ordered, and still there is room.' “
14.23: “And the owner said to his servant, 'Go out to the streets and to the place of hedges and compel them to enter, that my house may be filled.'
14.24: For I say to you that none of those men who were invited will partake of my supper.' “
14.25: And as there were great crowds going with him, he turned and he said to them:
14.26: “Whoever comes to me and does not hate his father and his mother and his brothers and his sisters and his wife and his children and even himself, he cannot be my disciple.”
14.27: “And whoever does not take his cross and come after me, cannot be my disciple.”
14.28: “For who among you, who wants to build a tower, does not first sit down and calculate its cost, if he is able to finish it,”
14.29: “Lest when he lays the foundation and cannot finish, all who see will mock him,”
14.30: “And they say, 'This man began to build and could not finish?”
14.31: “Or who is the King who goes to battle to fight with a neighboring King and does not first consider whether he can with 10,000 confront him who comes against him with 20,000?”
14.32: “And if not, while he is far off from him, he sends envoys and pleads for peace.”
14.33: “So none of you who does not forsake all his possessions can be my disciple.”
14.34: “Salt is excellent, but if even the salt becomes insipid, with what will it be seasoned?”
14.35: “It is not fit for land nor is it fit for manure, but they cast it outside. Whoever has an ear that hears, let him hear.”

15.1: But Tax Collectors and sinners were approaching to hear him.
15.2: And the Scribes and Pharisees were complaining and they were saying, “This one receives sinners, and he eats with them.”
15.3: And Yeshua told them this parable:
15.4: “What man among you has a hundred sheep, and if one of them should be lost, would not leave the ninety nine in the wilderness and go seek that one which is lost until he would find it?”
15.5: “And whenever he has found it he rejoices and carries it on his shoulder.”
15.6: “And he comes to his house and he calls his friends and his neighbors and he says to them, “Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep which was lost.”
15.7: “I say to you that there shall be joy like this in Heaven over one sinner who returns home, more than over ninety nine righteous ones who do not need a homecoming.”
15.8: “And who is the woman who has ten quarter shekels and will lose one of them and does not light a lamp and sweep the house and search for it carefully until she finds it?”
15.9: “When she has found it, she calls her friends and her neighbors, and she says to them, 'Rejoice with me, for I found my quarter shekel that was lost.' “
15.10: “I say to you that there will be joy like this before the Angels of God over one sinner who returns home.”
15.11: And Yeshua said to them again, “One man had two sons.”
15.12: “And his younger son said to him, 'My father, give me the portion that befalls me from your estate.' Then he divided to them his wealth.”
15.13: “And after a few days his younger son gathered everything that was coming to him and he went to a distant country, and there he squandered his wealth while living wastefully.”
15.14: “And when he had spent everything that he had, there was a great famine in that country and he began to be wanting.”
15.15: And he joined himself to one of the citizens of that country, and he sent him to a field to herd pigs.
15.16: And he longed to fill his belly from the carob pods that the pigs were eating, and no man was giving anything to him.
15.17: And when he came to himself, he said, 'Now, how many hired servants are in my father's house who have plenteous bread for themselves, and here I am dying with hunger!'
15.18: 'I shall arise and go to my father and say to him, “My father, I have sinned toward Heaven, and before you.'
15.19: “Now I am not worthy to be called your son. Make me as one of your hired servants.”
15.20: “And rising, he came to his father, and while he was still distant, his father saw him and he was moved with compassion for him and he ran and fell on his neck and he kissed him.”
15.21: And his son said to him, “My father, I have sinned toward Heaven and before you, and I am not worthy that I should be called your son.”
15.22: But his father said to his servants, 'Bring the best robe and clothe him and put a ring on his hand and put shoes on him.'
15.23: 'Bring and kill the fattened ox; let us eat and celebrate.'
15.24: 'Because this, my son, was dead, and he is alive; he was lost, and now he is found.' And they began to celebrate.
15.25: But his older son was in the field and as he came, he approached the house and he heard the sound of many people singing.
15.26: He called one of the boys and he asked him, “What is this?”
15.27: And he said to him, “Your brother has come, and your father has killed the fattened ox, because he has received him well.”
15.28: And he was angry and he did not want to enter and his father went out pleading with him.
15.29: But he said to his father, “Behold, I have served you for many years of servitude, and I have never transgressed your command, and you never gave a goat to me that I may celebrate with my friends.”
15.30: “But when this your son, who had wasted your money with whores, has come back, you killed for him the fattened ox.”
15.31: His father said to him, “My son, you are always with me and everything that I have is yours.”
15.32: “But it is right for us to celebrate and to rejoice. For this your brother was dead, and he is alive. He was lost, and he is found.”

16.1: And he told a parable to his disciples: “There was a certain rich man who had a steward, and they were accusing him that he was squandering his wealth.”
16.2: And his Lord summoned him and said to him, “What is this I have heard about you? Give me an inventory of your stewardship, for you cannot be a steward for me any longer.”
16.3: That steward said to himself, “What shall I do? My Lord has taken the stewardship from me; I cannot dig, and I am ashamed to beg.”
16.4: “I know what I shall do, that when I am put out from the stewardship, they may receive me into their houses.”
16.5: “And he called each one who was indebted to his lord and he said to the first, 'How much do you owe to my lord?' “
16.6: “And he said to him, 'A hundred baths of oil', and he said to him, 'Take your book, sit quickly and write fifty baths.' “
16.7: “And he said to another, 'And what do you owe to my lord?', and he said to him, 'A hundred cors of wheat.' He said to him, 'Take your book and sit and write eighty cors.' “
16.8: And our Lord praised the evil steward, because he acted wisely, for “The children of this world in this their generation are wiser than the children of light.”
16.9: “Also, I say to you, make for yourselves friends of this money of evil, that whenever it has been spent, they may receive you into their eternal dwellings.”
16.10: “Whoever is faithful with little is faithful with much also, and whoever does evil with a little also does evil with much”
16.11: “If therefore you are not faithful with the wealth of evil, who will commit to you the reality?”
16.12: “And if you are not found faithful with what is not yours, who will give you what is yours?”
16.13: “There is no servant who can serve two masters, for either he will hate the one and love the other, or he will honor the one and he will neglect the other. You cannot serve God and money.”
16.14: But when the Pharisees heard all these things, they were mocking him because they loved money.
16.15: But Yeshua said to them: “You are those who justify themselves before men, but God knows your hearts, for the thing that is exalted among men is disgusting before God.”
16.16: “The Law and The Prophets were until Yohannan; from then, the Kingdom of God is announced, and everyone is pushing to enter it.”
16.17: “But it is easier for Heaven and earth to pass away than for one symbol of The Law to pass away.”
16.18: “Whoever divorces his wife and takes another commits adultery, and everyone who takes her who is divorced commits adultery.”
16.19: “There was a certain rich man, and he wore fine white linen and purple and everyday he celebrated luxuriously.”
16.20: “And there was a certain poor man whose name was Lazar and he lay at the gate of that rich man, being stricken with abscesses.”
16.21: “And he longed to fill his belly with the fragments that fell from the rich man's table, but also the dogs would come licking his abscesses.”
16.22: “But that poor man died and Angels brought him to The Bosom of Abraham. And the rich man also died and he was buried.”
16.23: “And suffering in Sheol, he lifted up his eyes from afar off and he saw Abraham, and Lazar in his bosom.”
16.24: “And he called in a loud voice and he said, 'My father, Abraham, have pity on me and send Lazar to dip the tip of his finger in water and moisten my tongue for me; behold, I am suffering in this flame.' “
16.25: “Abraham said to him, 'My son, remember that you have received your good things in your life and Lazar, his evil things, and now, behold, he is comforted here and you are suffering.' “
16.26: 'And along with all these things, there stands a great abyss between us and you, so that those who would pass from here to you are not able, neither is whoever is there able to pass over to us.'
16.27: “He said to him, 'Therefore, I beg of you, my father, to send him to my father's house.' “
16.28: 'For I have five brothers; he should go testify to them so that they would not come to this place of torment also.'
16.29: “Abraham said to him, 'They have Moses and The Prophets, let them hear them.' “
16.30: “But he said to him, 'No, my father Abraham, but if a man will go to them from the dead, they will be converted.' “
16.31: “Abraham said to him, 'If they will not hear Moses and The Prophets, they will not believe him, even though a man would rise from the dead.' “

17.1: And Yeshua said to his disciples, “It is not possible that offenses shall not come, but woe to him by whose hand they shall come.”
17.2: “It were better for him if the millstone of a donkey were hung on his neck and he were cast into the sea than that he would subvert one of these little ones.”
17.3: “Guard your souls. If your brother should sin, rebuke him, and if he repents, forgive him.”
17.4: “And if he sins against you seven times in a day and seven times in the day returns to you and says, 'I am sorry', forgive him.”
17.5: And The Apostles said to Our Lord, “Increase our faith.”
17.6: He said to them, “If you have faith like a grain of mustard seed, you may say to this sycamore tree, 'Be uprooted and be planted in the sea', and it would obey you.”
17.7: “But who among you has a servant who drives a plow or who tends to sheep, and if he would come from the field, would say to him at once, 'Go on; recline for supper?”
17.8: “But he says to him, 'Prepare for me something to eat and put on your apron to serve me until I shall have eaten and have drunk; after this you also will eat and drink.'”
17.9: “Does that servant receive his thanks because he did the thing that he was commanded? I think not.”
17.10: “So also you, whenever you have done all those things that were commanded you, you should say, 'We are unprofitable servants, because we have done that which we were obligated to do.' “
17.11: And it was that as Yeshua went to Jerusalem, he passed through among Samaritans to Galilee.
17.12: And when he approached to enter a certain village, ten men who were lepers met him, and they stood afar off.
17.13: And they lifted up their voices and they were saying, “Our Rabbi, Yeshua, have mercy on us!”
17.14: And when he saw them, he said to them, “Go show yourselves to the priests”, and as they were going, they were purified.
17.15: But one of them, when he saw that he was purified, he returned to him and he was praising God with a loud voice.
17.16: And he fell on his face before the feet of Yeshua as he gave thanks to him, and this one was a Samaritan.
17.17: But Yeshua answered and he said, “Were there not ten who were purified? Where are the nine?”
17.18: “Have they neglected to come give glory to God, except this one who is from a foreign people?”
17.19: And he said to him, “Arise; go. Your faith has saved you.”
17.20: And when some of the Pharisees asked Yeshua, “When is the Kingdom of God coming”, he answered and he said to them, “The Kingdom of God does not come with what is observed.”
17.21: “Neither do they say, 'Behold, here it is!' and 'Behold, from here to there!', for behold, the Kingdom of God is within some of you.”
17.22: And he said to his disciples, “The days will come when you shall long to see one of the days of The Son of Man, and you shall not see.”
17.23: “And if they will say to you, 'Behold, he is here.' and 'Behold he is there', do not go.”
17.24: “For just as lightning flashes from the sky and lights up all beneath the sky, thus shall The Son of Man be in his day.”
17.25: “But first, he is going to suffer many things and he shall be rejected by this generation.”
17.26: “And as it was in the days of Noah, thus shall it be the days of The Son of Man.”
17.27: “For they were eating and drinking and taking wives and giving them to men until the day that Noah entered the ark and the flood came and destroyed everyone.”
17.28: “And again, just as it was in the days of Lot, they were eating and drinking and buying and selling and planting and building.”
17.29: “But in the day that Lot went out from Sadom, THE LORD JEHOVAH caused it to rain fire and brimstone from the sky and destroyed all of them.”
17.30: “Thus shall it be in the day when The Son of Man is revealed.”
17.31: “In that day, whoever is on the roof, and his stuff in the house, let him not come down to take them. And whoever is in the field, let him not turn back.”
17.33: “Whoever chooses to save his life shall lose it, and whoever will lose his life shall find it.”
17.34: “I say to you, in that night, two shall be in one bed; one shall be taken captive and the other shall be left.”
17.35: “Two women shall be grinding meal together; one shall be led away captive and the other shall be left.”
17.36: “Two shall be in a field; one shall be taken captive and the other shall be left.”
17.37: And they answered and they were saying to him, “To where, Our Lord?” He said to them, “Wherever the bodies are, there the eagles shall be gathered.”

18.1: He told them also a parable that they should pray at all times and they should not grow weary:
18.2: “There was in one city a certain judge who did not worship God and did not honor the children of men.”
18.3: “There was a certain widow in that city, and she was coming to him and saying, 'Vindicate me of my legal adversary.' “
18.4: “And he would not for long time, but after this, he said to himself, 'Although I do not worship God neither honor people'
18.5: 'Nevertheless, because this widow wearies me, I shall avenge her, lest she be constantly coming and annoying me.' “
18.6: And our Lord said, “Hear what the evil judge said.”
18.7: “Shall not God all the more perform vindication for his Elect, who cry to him by day and by night, and he delays his Spirit concerning them?”
18.8: “I say to you that he shall perform their vindication quickly. However, The Son of Man shall come, and will he then find faith on the earth?”
18.9: And he told this parable against those men who trusted in themselves that they were righteous, and they held contempt for everyone:
18.10: “Two men went up to The Temple to pray: one a Pharisee and the other a Tax Collector.”
18.11: The Pharisee was standing alone by himself and he was praying these things: “God, I thank you that I am not like other men: extortioners, oppressors, adulterers, and not like this Tax Collector.”
18.12: “But I fast twice in a week and I tithe everything that I possess.”
18.13: “But that Tax Collector was standing from a distance and he would not even lift his eyes up to Heaven, but he was smiting on his chest and he said, 'God, have pity on me, a sinner.' “
18.14: “I say to you that this one went down to his house justified rather than that Pharisee, for every man who exalts himself shall be humbled, and everyone who humbles himself shall be exalted.”
18.15: But they brought him also infants that he might touch them, and his disciples saw them, and they rebuked them.
18.16: But Yeshua himself called them and he said to them, “Let the children come to me and do not refuse them, because the Kingdom of Heaven belongs to those who are like these.”
18.17: “Amen, I say to you, whoever does not receive the Kingdom of God as a little boy shall not enter it.”
18.18: And one who was a nobleman asked him and said to him, “Good teacher, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?”
18.19: Yeshua said to him, “Why do you call me good? There is no one good except the one God.”
18.20: “You know the commandments: 'You shall not murder. You shall not commit adultery. You shall not steal. You shall not testify a false testimony. Honor your father and your mother.' “
18.21: He said to him, “I have kept all these things from my childhood.”
18.22: But when Yeshua heard these things, he said to him, “You lack one thing: Go sell everything that you have and give to the poor, and you shall have a treasure in Heaven, and come after me.”
18.23: But when he heard these things, it grieved him, for he was very rich.
18.24: And when Yeshua saw that it grieved him, he said, “How hard it is for those who have wealth to enter the Kingdom of God.”
18.25: “It is easier for a camel to enter the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter the Kingdom of God.”
18.26: Those who heard were saying to him, “And who can have life?”
18.27: But Yeshua said, “Those things which are impossible with men can happen with God.”
18.28: Shimeon Kaypha said to him, “Behold, we have left everything and we have come after you.”
18.29: Yeshua said to him, “Truly, I say to you, there is no man who has left house or parents or brother or wife or children for the sake of the Kingdom of God,”
18.30: “Who shall not receive in great multiples in this time, and in the coming world, eternal life.”
18.31: And Yeshua led his twelve and he said to them, “Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem and all that is written in The Prophets about The Son of Man shall be fulfilled.”
18.32: “For he shall be delivered to Gentiles, and they shall mock him and they shall spit in his face.”
18.33: “They shall scourge him, they shall abuse him and they shall kill him, and on the third day he shall rise.”
18.34: But they understood none of these things and this saying was hidden from them and they did not know these things that were spoken with them.
18.35: And when they came near to Jericho, a certain blind man was sitting on the side of the road and begging.
18.36: And he heard the sound of the crowd that passed by and he was asking, “Who is this?”
18.37: They were saying to him, “Yeshua the Nazarene passes by.”
18.38: And he cried out and he said, “Yeshua, Son of David, have mercy on me!”
18.39: And those who were going in front of Yeshua were rebuking him that he should be quiet, but he was crying out even more, “Son of David, have mercy on me!”
18.40: And Yeshua stood still, and he commanded to bring him to him, and when he came near to him, he asked him,
18.41: And he said to him, “What do you want me to do for you?” But he said, “My Lord, that I may see.”
18.42: And Yeshua said to him, “See; your faith has saved you.”
18.43: And immediately he saw, and he was coming after him and he was praising God, and all the people who saw were giving glory to God.

19.1: And when Yeshua entered and passed through Jericho,
19.2: A certain man was there whose name was Zakkai; he was a rich man and Chief of Tax Collectors,
19.3: And he wanted to see who Yeshua was and he was not able to from the crowd because Zakkai was small in stature.
19.4: And he ran before it to Yeshua and he climbed up a bare fig tree to see him because he was going to pass by there.
19.5: And when he came to that place, Yeshua saw him and said to him, “Hasten, come down, Zakkai, for today I must stay at your house.”
19.6: And he made haste and he came down and received him rejoicing.
19.7: But when all of them saw it, they were all complaining and they were saying, “He entered and lodged with a man that is a sinner.”
19.8: Then Zakkai arose and he said to Yeshua, “Behold, my Lord, I give half my wealth to the poor, and anything that I have seized I repay fourfold to every man.”
19.9: Yeshua said to him, “Today, The Life has come to this house, because This One also is The Son of Abraham.”
19.10: “For The Son of Man has come to seek and to save that which was lost.”
19.11: And as they heard these things, he added to speak a parable because he was nearing Jerusalem, and they were expecting in that same hour that the Kingdom of God was going to be revealed.
19.12: And he said, “A certain man, a son of great descent, went to a distant region to receive a Kingdom and to return.”
19.13: And he called his ten servants and he gave them ten minas and he said to them, “Invest in trading until I come.”
19.14: But the citizens of the city hated him and they sent envoys after him and they were saying, “We do not want this man to reign over us.”
19.15: And when he had received the Kingdom and returned, he said to summon to him his servants to whom he had given money that he may know what everyone of them had traded.
19.16: The first came and he said, “My Lord, your mina has gained ten minas.”
19.17: He said to him, “Excellent, good servant! Because you are found faithful with a little, you shall be a ruler over ten fortress cities.”
19.18: The second came and he said, “My Lord, your mina has gained five minas.”
19.19: He said also to this one, “You also shall be ruler over five fortress cities.”
19.20: Another came and he said, “My lord, behold, that mina, which I had is now laid in fine linen.”
19.21: “For I was afraid of you, for you are a hard man, for you take up that which you have not laid down and you reap the thing which you have not sown.”
19.22: He said to him, “I will judge you from your own mouth, you evil servant, for you had known me that I am a hard man, and I take up the thing that I have not laid down, and I reap the thing that I have not sown.
19.23: Why did you not put my money upon the exchange and I would have come to seek it with its interest?”
19.24: And he said to those who stood before him, “Take from him the mina and give to him who has ten minas with him.”
19.25: And they were saying to him, “Our Lord, he has ten minas.”
19.26: He said to them, “I tell you that everyone who has it, it shall be given him, and from him who does not have it, even that which he has shall be taken from him.”
19.27: “However, those my enemies, who did not want me to reign over them, bring them and kill them in front of me.”
19.28: And when Yeshua had said these things, he went out to those before him to go to Jerusalem.
19.29: And when he arrived at Bethphage and Bethany on the side of the Mount which is called 'Bayth Zaytay', he sent two of his disciples.
19.30: He said to them, “Go to the village which is opposite us, and when you enter, behold, you shall find a colt tied on which a man has never ridden; loose and bring him.”
19.31: “If a man asks you why you are loosing him, say thus to him: 'He is needed for Our Lord.' “
19.32: And they who were sent went and found just as he had said to them.
19.33: And as they loosed the colt, its owners were saying to them, “Why are you loosing that colt?”
19.34: And they said to them, “He is needed for Our Lord.”
19.35: And they brought him to Yeshua and they cast their garments on the colt and set Yeshua upon him.
19.36: As he went they were spreading their garments in the road.
19.37: When he approached the descent of the Mount of Bayth Zaytay, all the crowds of disciples began rejoicing and praising God with loud voices for all the mighty works that they had seen.
19.38: And they were saying, “Blessed is The King who comes in the name of THE LORD JEHOVAH; peace in Heaven and glory in The Highest.”
19.39: But some of the Pharisees from among the crowds were saying to him, “Rabbi, rebuke your disciples.”
19.40: He said to them, “I say to you that if these would be silent, the stones would be crying out loud.”
19.41: And when he came near and he saw the city he wept over it.
19.42: And he said, “If only now you had known those things that are of your peace, if even in this your day! But now these things are hidden from your eyes.”
19.43: “The days shall come to you when your enemies shall surround you and they shall press you in from every side.”
19.44: “And they shall destroy you and your children within you and they shall not leave you one stone standing on another, because you did not know the time of your visitation.”
19.45: And when he entered The Temple he began to cast out those who bought and sold in it.
19.46: And he said to them, “It is written: 'My house is the house of prayer', but you have made it a den of robbers.'”
19.47: He was teaching everyday in The Temple, but the Chief Priests and the Scribes and the Elders of the people were seeking to destroy him.
19.48: And they could not find what they might do to him, for all the people were hanging on him to hear him.

20.1: And it happened on one of the days while he was teaching the people in The Temple and evangelizing, there stood about him Chief Priests and Scribes with Elders.
20.2: They were saying to him, “Tell us by what authority do you do these things, and who is he who has given you this authority?”
20.3: Yeshua answered and he said to them, “I shall also ask you a question and you answer me.”
20.4: “Was the baptism of Yohannan from Heaven, or from men?”
20.5: But they were counseling among themselves and they were saying, “If we say, 'From Heaven', he will say to us, 'And why did you not believe him?' “
20.6: “But if we say, 'From men', all the people will stone us, for they are convinced that Yohannan is a Prophet.”
20.7: And they said to him, “We do not know from where it was.”
20.8: Yeshua said to them, “Neither do I say to you by what authority I do these things.”
20.9: And he began to tell the people this parable: “A certain man planted a vineyard and handed it over to laborers and he went abroad for a long time.”
20.10: And at the season he sent his servant to the workers to give him some of the fruits of the vineyard, but the workers beat him and they sent him away stripped.
20.11: And he added and sent another servant, but they beat that one also and they abused him and they sent him away naked.
20.12: But he added and sent a third, but they also wounded that one and cast him out.
20.13: The owner of the vineyard said, 'What shall I do? I shall send my beloved son. Doubtless they will see him and they will be ashamed.'
20.14: “But when the workers saw him, they were counseling among themselves and they were saying, 'This is the heir; come let us kill him, and the inheritance shall be ours.' “
20.15: “And they cast him out of the vineyard and murdered him. What therefore will the owner of the vineyard do to them?”
20.16: “He shall come and destroy those workers, and he shall give the vineyard to others”; but when they heard, they said, “May this not be!”
20.17: But he gazed upon them and he said, “And what is this that is written: 'The stone which the builders rejected has become the head of the corner'”?
20.18: “And everything that falls on that stone shall be broken, and it will scatter everything whatsoever upon which it will fall.”
20.19: But the Chief Priests and the Scribes were seeking to lay hands upon him at that hour, and they were afraid of the people, for they knew that he had spoken this parable against them.
20.20: And they sent spies who imitated righteous men to catch him in discourse and to deliver him to the Judge and to the authority of the Governor.
20.21: And they asked him, and they were saying to him, “Teacher, we know that you are speaking and teaching correctly, and you do not show favoritism, but you teach the way of God in truth.”
20.22: “Is it legal for us to give poll tax to Caesar or not?”
20.23: “But he perceived their cunning and he said, “Why are you testing me?”
20.24: “Show me a denarius. Whose image and inscription is on it?” They said, “Caesar's.”
20.25: Yeshua said to them, “Give therefore to Caesar what is Caesar's, and to God what is God's.”
20.26: And they could not lay hold of any word from him before the people, and they were amazed at his answer, and they kept silent.
20.27: But some of the Sadducees came, those who say that there is no resurrection, and they asked him,
20.28: And they were saying to him, “Teacher, Moses wrote to us that if a man dies and his brother has a wife without sons, his brother shall take his wife, and he shall raise up a son to his brother.”
20.29: “But there were seven brothers and the first took a wife and he died without sons.”
20.30: “And the second took her for his wife, and he died without sons.”
20.31: “And the third again took her and thus also the seven of them, and they died and left no sons.”
20.33: In the resurrection therefore, whose wife will she be, for the seven of them married her?
20.34: Yeshua said to them, “The sons of this world take women and women are given to men.
20.35: But those who are worthy for that world and for the resurrection from among the dead are not taking women, neither are women taking men.
20.36: For neither can they die again, for they are like The Angels, and they are the children of God because they are the children of the resurrection.
20.37: But that the dead rise, Moses also declared, for he recounts at the bush, when THE LORD JEHOVAH said, “The God of Abraham and the God of Isaaq and the God of Jaqob.
20.38: But he was not the God of the dead, but of the living, for all of them were alive to him.”
20.39: And some of the Scribes answered, and they were saying to him, “Teacher, you have spoken beautifully.”
20.40: And they dared not ask him about anything again.
20.41: He said to them, “How do the Scribes say about The Messiah that he is the son of David?
20.42: And David said in the book of Psalms, 'THE LORD JEHOVAH said to my Lord, “Seat yourself at my right hand,
20.43: Until I put your enemies under your feet.'
20.44: If, therefore, David called him, 'my Lord', how is he his son?”
20.45: And while all the people heard, he said to his disciples:
20.46: “Beware the Scribes who like to walk in robes and love greetings in the markets and first class seats in the synagogues and first class rooms at banquets.
20.47: They who consume widows houses for an offering of prolonging their prayers; those shall receive an extreme judgment.”

21.1: But Yeshua gazed at the rich who were casting in their offerings to the treasury.
21.2: And he saw also a certain poor widow who cast in two shemonas.
21.3: And he said, “I tell you the reality, that this poor widow has cast in more than anyone.
21.4: For all of these have cast in whatever excess they had into the place of God's offerings, but this one from her want has cast in everything that she owned.”
21.5: And as people were talking about The Temple, that it was adorned with beautiful stones and with gifts, Yeshua said to them:
21.6: “Do you see these things? The days will come in which not one stone will be left standing upon another stone that shall not be pulled down.”
21.7: And they were asking him and they were saying, “Teacher, when will these things occur, and what is the sign that these things are close to occurring?”
21.8: But he said to them, “Beware that you be not deceived, for many shall come in my name, and they shall say, 'I am God, The Messiah', and the time is near, but do not go after them.
21.9: And whenever you hear wars and commotions, do not be afraid, for these things are going to happen first, but the end will not have yet arrived.
21.10: For nation shall arise against nation and Kingdom against Kingdom.
21.11: Great earthquakes shall be in various places, and there shall be famines and plagues and panics and terror, and great signs from the sky shall appear and there shall be great storms.
21.12: But before all these things they will lay hands on you and they shall persecute you and they shall deliver you to councils and prisons, and they shall bring you before Kings and Governors because of my name.
21.13: But it will happen to you for a testimony.
21.14: But settle in your hearts that you will not be taught to give an answer.
21.15: For I will give you a mouth and wisdom which all your enemies will not be able to withstand.
21.16: But your parents and your brothers and your relatives and your friends shall deliver you over, and they shall put some of you to death.
21.17: And you shall be hated by every man, because of my name.
21.18: A hair of your heads shall not be lost.
21.19: But by your perseverance you shall possess your souls.
21.20: But whenever you will see Jerusalem being surrounded by armies, know then that its destruction has come near to it.
21.21: And let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains, and those who are within it escape, and those in the villages, let them not enter into it.
21.22: These are the days of vengeance, to fulfill everything whatsoever that has been written.
21.23: But woe to those who are pregnant and those who nurse in those days, for there shall be great suffering in the land and wrath upon this people.
21.24: And they shall fall by the mouth of the sword and they shall be led captive to every region, and Jerusalem shall be trodden down by the Gentiles until the time of the Gentiles will be finished.
21.25: And there shall be signs in the sun and in the moon and in the stars and in the earth, suffering of nations and clasping of hands, from the alarm of the sound of the sea,
21.26: And an earthquake which drives out the souls of the children of men by the terror of whatever is going to come on the earth, and the hosts of the Heavens shall be shaken.
21.27: And then they shall see The Son of Man, who comes in clouds with many mighty works and great praises.
21.28: But whenever these things begin to happen, take heart and lift up your heads, because your salvation draws near.”
21.29: And he told them the parable: “Behold the fig tree and all of the trees.
21.30: When they bud, at once you understand by them that summer approaches.
21.31: Thus also whenever you see these things occurring, know that the Kingdom of God is near.
21.32: Amen, I say to you, this generation shall not pass until all of these things shall come to pass.
21.33: Heaven and Earth shall pass away and my words shall not pass away.
21.34: But beware in your souls that your hearts never grow cold with gluttony and with drunkenness and with the cares of the world, and suddenly it shall come upon you that day.
21.35: For it shall spring like a trap upon all of those who dwell upon the face of all the earth.
21.36: Be watching at all times, therefore, and praying that you will be worthy to escape from these things which are going to occur, and to stand before The Son of Man.”
21.37: And by day he was teaching in The Temple and by night he went out, spending the night in the mount which is called 'Bayth Zaytay'.
21.38: And all the people were coming early to him to The Temple to hear his word. Luke

22.1: Now The Feast of Unleavened Bread was nearing, which is called Passover.
22.2: And the Chief Priests and Scribes were seeking how they would kill him, but they were afraid of the people.
22.3: But Satan had entered Yehuda, who is called Skariota, he who had been of the number of the twelve.
22.4: And he went and spoke with the Chief Priests and the Scribes and the Commanders of the forces of The Temple so as to deliver him to them.
22.5: And they rejoiced and they promised to give him money.
22.6: And he made an agreement with them and he was seeking occasion to deliver him to them apart from a gathering.
22.7: The Day of Unleavened Bread arrived in which it was the custom for the Passover lamb to be slain.
22.8: And Yeshua sent Yohannan and Kaypha and he said to them, “Go prepare for us to eat the Passover.”
22.9: But they said to him, “Where do you want us to prepare it?”
22.10: He said to them, “Behold, when you enter the city, you shall meet a man who bears a jug of water; go after him.
22.11: And wherever he enters, say to the owner of the house, “Our Rabbi says, 'Is there a place of dwelling where I may eat Passover with my disciples?'
22.12: And behold, he shall show you a great furnished upper room; prepare there.”
22.13: And they went and found as he had told them, and they prepared the Passover.
22.14: And when it was time, Yeshua came and reclined and the twelve Apostles with him.
22.15: And he said to them, “I have greatly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer.
22.16: For I say to you, from now on, I shall not eat it until it shall be fulfilled in the Kingdom of God.”
22.17: And he took a cup, and he gave thanks and he said, “Take this and divide it among yourselves.
22.18: I say to you that I shall not drink from the fruit of the vine until the Kingdom of God shall come.”
22.19: He took bread and he gave thanks, he broke and he gave to them and he said, “This is my body, which shall be given for the sake of your persons. You shall be doing this to commemorate me.”
22.20: And thus also concerning the cup after they had dined, he said, “This cup is the new covenant in my blood, which shall be shed in your stead.
22.21: However, behold; the hand of him who shall betray me is on the table.
22.22: The Son of Man goes just as it was appointed, yet woe to that man by whose hand he shall be betrayed!”
22.23: And they began to inquire among themselves which one of them it was indeed who was going to commit this.
22.24: But there was also a dispute among them, of who among them was great.
22.25: Then Yeshua said to them, “The Kings of the nations are their lords and they who rule over them are called Benefactors.
22.26: But you are not so, for whoever is great among you shall be as the little man, and whoever is chief shall be like a waiter.
22.27: For who is greater: he who sits or he who is a waiter? Is it not he who sits? But I am among you as he who is a waiter.
22.28: But you are those who have remained with me in my trials.
22.29: And I am promising you a Kingdom as my Father promised me.
22.30: For you shall eat and you shall drink at the table of my Kingdom, and you shall sit on thrones, and you shall judge the twelve tribes of Israel.”
22.31: And Yeshua said to Shimeon, “Shimeon, behold, Satan has requested that he may sift you all like wheat,”
22.32: And I have prayed for you that your faith will not fail, and when you are restored, confirm your brothers.”
22.33: But Shimeon said to him, “My Lord, I am ready for prison and for death with you.”
22.34: Yeshua said to him, “I say to you, Shimeon, that a rooster shall not crow today until you shall deny three times that you know me.”
22.35: And he said to them, “When I sent you without a money bag and without wallet and shoes, did you lack anything? And they were saying to him, “Nothing!”
22.36: He said to them, “From this hour, whoever has a money bag should take it and thus also a wallet, and whoever lacks a sword, let him sell his tunic and buy a sword for himself.
22.37: For I say unto you, this also that is written must be fulfilled in me, 'He was numbered with the evil doers', for all that concerns me shall be fulfilled.”
22.38: And they were saying to him, 'Our Lord, behold, here are two swords.' He said to them, 'They are enough.'
22.39: And he went out and he went on as he was accustomed to the Mount Bayth Zaytay and his disciples also went after him.
22.40: And when he arrived at the place, he said to them, “Pray, lest you enter temptation.”
22.41: And he withdrew from them about a stone's throw and he bowed his knees and he prayed.
22.42: And he said, “Father, if you are willing, let this cup pass from me; however not my will, but yours be done.”
22.43: And an Angel appeared to him from Heaven, who strengthened him.
22.44: And as he was in fear, he prayed urgently, and his sweat was like drops of blood, and he fell upon the ground.
22.45: And he arose from his prayer and he came to his disciples and found them sleeping from the anguish.
22.46: And he said to them, “Why are you sleeping? Arise; pray, lest you enter temptation.”
22.47: And while he was speaking, behold, a crowd and he who is called Yehuda, one of the twelve, came. He went before them, and he came to Yeshua and he kissed him, for he had given a sign to them: “Whomever I kiss is the one.”
22.48: Yeshua said to him, “Yehuda, do you betray The Son of Man with a kiss?”
22.49: But when those who were with him saw what happened, they were saying to him, “Our Lord, shall we strike them with swords?”
22.50: And one of them struck the servant of The High Priest and took off his right ear.
22.51: Then Yeshua answered and he said, “Enough for now.” And he touched the ear of him who was wounded, and he healed him.
22.52: And Yeshua said to those Chief Priests and Elders and Commanders of the army of The Temple, who had come upon him, “Have you come out for me as for a robber, with swords and with clubs to seize me?
22.53: I was with you every day in The Temple and you did not stretch forth your hands against me, but this is your hour and of the Prince of Darkness.”
22.54: And they seized him and brought him to the house of The High Priest, and Shimeon was coming after him from a distance.
22.55: But they kindled a fire in the midst of the courtyard and they were sitting around it and Shimeon was also sitting among them.
22.56: And a certain Maidservant saw him as he sat at the fire and she stared at him and she said, “This man also was with him.”
22.57: But he denied it and he said, “Woman, I do not know him.”
22.58: After a little while another saw him and he said to him, “You also are one of them.”,but Kaypha said, “I am not.”
22.59: And after an hour another was disputing and he said, “Surely this man also was with him, for he is also a Galilean.”
22.60: Kaypha said, “Man, I do not know what you are saying”, and at once, while he was speaking, a rooster crowed.
22.61: And Yeshua turned and he gazed at Kaypha, and Shimeon remembered the saying of our Lord that he had told him: “Before a rooster shall crow, you shall deny me three times.”
22.62: And Shimeon went outside and wept bitterly.
22.63: And the men who were holding Yeshua were mocking him, and they were covering him
22.64: And striking him on his face, and they were saying, “Prophesy, who is hitting you?”
22.65: And they were uttering many other blasphemies and they were speaking against him.
22.66: And when day had dawned, the Elders and the Chief Priests and the Scribes were gathered together and they brought him to the place of their assembly.
22.67: And they were saying to him, “If you are The Messiah, tell us.” He said to them, “If I tell you, you will not believe me.
22.68: And if I will ask you, you will not give me an answer, neither will you release me.
22.69: Henceforth The Son of Man will be sitting at the right hand of the power of God.”
22.70: But all of them were saying, “You are therefore the Son of God?” Yeshua said to them, “You are saying that I AM THE LIVING GOD.”
22.71: And they were saying, “Why do we need witnesses again, for we hear from his own mouth?”

23.1: And their whole gathering arose and brought him to Pilate.
23.2: And they began slandering him and they were saying, “We found this one seducing our people and he forbids giving head tax to Caesar, and he has said about himself that he is The King Messiah.”
23.3: Then Pilate asked him and he said to him, “You are The King of the Judeans?” He said to him, “You have said.”
23.4: And Pilate said to the Chief Priests and to the crowd, “I find no fault concerning this man.”
23.5: But they were shouting and saying, “He has stirred up our people and he taught in all Judea and began from Galilee even unto here.”
23.6: But when Pilate heard the name of Galilee, he asked if the man were a Galilean.
23.7: And when he knew that he was under the authority of Herodus, he sent him to the presence of Herodus, because he was in Jerusalem in those days.
23.8: Now when Herodus saw Yeshua, he was very glad, for he had wanted to see him for a long time, because he had heard many things about him and he had hoped to see some sign from him.
23.9: And he was asking him of many matters but Yeshua gave him no answer.
23.10: But the Chief Priests and the Scribes were standing and were vehemently accusing him.
23.11: But Herodus and his servants mocked him, and when he had insulted him, he clothed him with a purple robe and he sent him to Pilate.
23.12: And on that day, Pilate and Herodus became friends with each other, because there had been hostility between them before that.
23.13: Then Pilate called the Chief Priests and the Rulers of the people,
23.14: And he said to them, “You brought me this man as subverting your people, and behold I have examined him in your sight, and I find no fault in this man in anything of which you are accusing him;
23.15: Neither has Herodus, for I sent him to him, and nothing worthy of death has been committed by him.
23.16: Therefore I shall discipline him and release him.”
23.17: For it was a custom to release them one prisoner at the feast.
23.18: But the entire mob shouted, and they were saying, “Take this one away and release us Barabba”,
23.19: Who, because of a sedition and murder that had occurred in that city, had been cast into prison.
23.20: Pilate spoke with them again as he wanted to release Yeshua.
23.21: They were shouting and saying, “Crucify him! Crucify him!”
23.22: The third time, he said to them, “What evil has this one done? I have not found any fault in him that deserves death. I shall chastise him, therefore, and I shall release him.”
23.23: But they were urging him in a loud voice and demanding that they would crucify him, and their voice prevailed, and that of the Chief Priests.
23.24: And Pilate commanded that their demand should be performed.
23.25: And he released to them him who had been cast into prison for sedition and murder, whom they had demanded, but he delivered Yeshua to their pleasure.
23.26: And as they brought him, they seized Shimeon a Cyrenian who came from a village and they laid upon him the cross to carry after Yeshua.
23.27: And a multitude of people came after him and those women who were lamenting and howling over him.
23.28: And Yeshua turned to them, and he said, “Daughters of Jerusalem, do not weep for me, but weep for yourselves and for your children.
23.29: For the days are coming in which they shall say, 'Blessed are the barren and the wombs that that have not borne, and the breasts that did not nurse.'
23.30: And they shall begin to say to the mountains, 'Fall upon us', and to the hills, 'Cover us.'
23.31: For if they are doing these things with green wood, what will happen with the dried?”
23.32: And there were two others coming with him, evildoers to be killed.
23.33: And when they came to a certain place called Qarqpatha, they crucified him there and those evildoers, one at his right, and one at his left.
23.34: But Yeshua was saying, “Father, forgive them, for they know not what they do.” And they divided his garments, and they cast lots for them.
23.35: But the people were standing and watching and the leaders were mocking him and they were saying, “He gave life to others; let him save himself if he is The Messiah, The Chosen One of God.”
23.36: And the soldiers were also scoffing at him as they came near to him and they were offering him vinegar.
23.37: And they were saying to him, “If you are The King of the Judeans, save yourself.”
23.38: And there was an inscription that was written over him in Greek and in Latin and in Aramaic: “This is The King of the Judeans.”
23.39: But one of those evildoers who were crucified with him was blaspheming him and he said, “If you are The Messiah, save yourself and save us also.”
23.40: And his companion rebuked him and he said to him, “Are you not even afraid of God? For you also are in condemnation with him.
23.41: And we justly so, because we are worthy, for we are repaid according to what we have done, but nothing evil has been done by this one.”
23.42: And he said to Yeshua, “My Lord, remember me when you come into your Kingdom.”
23.43: But Yeshua said to him, “Amen, I say to you that today you shall be with me in Paradise.”
23.44: But it was about the sixth hour and there was darkness upon all the earth until the ninth hour.
23.45: And the sun grew dark, and the curtain of The Temple was ripped apart from its middle.
23.46: And Yeshua called out in a loud voice and he said, “My Father, into your hands I lay down my spirit.” He said this and he expired.
23.47: When the Centurion saw what had happened, he glorified God and he said, “Truly this man was The Righteous One.”
23.48: And all the crowds which had gathered for this spectacle, when they saw what had happened, returned while smiting on their chests.
23.49: And all the acquaintances of Yeshua and those women who had come with him from Galilee were standing afar off and they were beholding these things.
23.50: A certain man whose name was Yoseph, a Sanhedrin member from Ramtha, a city of Judea, was a good man and just.
23.51: This one had not consented to their decision and to their action, and he was waiting for the Kingdom of God.
23.52: This man came to Pilate, and he asked for the body of Yeshua.
23.53: And he took it down and wrapped it in a winding sheet of linen, and he placed it in a cut out tomb in which no one had yet been placed.
23.54: The day was Friday and the Sabbath was beginning.
23.55: But these women who came with him from Galilee were approaching and they saw the tomb and how his body had been laid.
23.56: And they returned, and prepared sweet spices and ointment, and on the Sabbath they rested according to that which had been commanded.

24.1: But on Sunday morning, while it was dark, they came to the tomb and they brought the spices that they had prepared, and there were other women with them.
24.2: And they found the stone which had been rolled from the tomb.
24.3: And they entered and they did not find the body of Yeshua.
24.4: And it happened that while these wondered at this, behold, two men stood above them and their clothing was shining.
24.5: And they were in fear and they bowed their faces to the ground, and they were saying to them, “Why are you seeking The Life among the dead?
24.6: He is not here; he is risen. Remember when he spoke with you as he was in Galilee,
24.7: And he said, 'The Son of Man is going to be delivered into the hands of sinful men, and he shall be crucified and the third day he shall arise?' “
24.9: And they returned from the tomb and they were telling all these things to the eleven and to the rest.
24.10: And they were Maryam Magdalitha and Johanna and Maryam the mother of Yaqob, and the rest who were with them, who told these things to the Apostles.
24.11: And these words appeared as insanity in their eyes and they did not believe them.
24.12: But Shimeon arose and he ran to the tomb, and beholding, he saw the linen that was placed by itself and he left wondering in his soul over what had happened.
24.13: And behold, two of them that day were going to the village whose name is Emmaus, and it is sixty furlongs from Jerusalem.
24.14: And they were speaking with one another about all these things that had occurred.
24.15: And while they were talking and inquiring one with another, Yeshua came and he met them and he was walking with them.
24.16: And their eyes had been held shut lest they would recognize him.
24.17: And he said to them, “What are these matters of which you speak, one with another, as you are walking and are gloomy?”
24.18: One of them answered, whose name was Cleopa, and he said to him, “Are you indeed a foreigner by yourself in Jerusalem that you do not know the thing that has occurred in it in these days?”
24.19: He said to them, “What thing?” They were saying to him, “Concerning Yeshua, who was from Nazareth, a man who was The Prophet mighty in word and in deed before God, and before the whole nation.
24.20: And the Chief Priests and the Elders delivered him to the sentence of death, and they crucified him.
24.21: But we had been hoping that he was going to deliver Israel and behold, it is the third day now, since all these things occurred.
24.22: But also women among us stupefied us, for they had gone first to the tomb.
24.23: And when they did not find his body, they came and they were telling us, 'We saw Angels there', and they were saying of him, 'He is alive.'
24.24: Also, some from us went to the tomb and they found according to what the women said, but they did not see him.”
24.25: Then Yeshua said to them, “Oh deficient of mind and slow of heart to believe in all the things that The Prophets have spoken!
24.26: Was it not necessary for The Messiah to endure these things and to enter into his glory?”
24.27: And he began from Moses and from all The Prophets and he expounded to them about himself from all of the scriptures.
24.28: And they arrived at that village to which they were going, and he was announcing to them how he was going to a distant place.
24.29: And they constrained him and they were saying to him, “Stay with us, because the day is declining and it is becoming dark”, and he entered to stay with them.
24.30: And it happened that when he reclined with them, he took bread and he blessed, and he broke and he gave to them.
24.31: At once their eyes were opened and they recognized him and he ascended from them.
24.32: And they were saying one to another, “Was not our heart dull within us when he was speaking with us on the road and expounding to us the scriptures?”
24.33: And they arose in that hour, and they returned to Jerusalem, and they found the eleven assembled and those who were with them,
24.34: As they were saying, “Truly our Lord has risen and he has appeared to Shimeon!”
24.35: They also related those things that had occurred on the road and how he was known to them when he broke the bread.
24.36: And when they were speaking these things, Yeshua stood in their midst and he said to them, “Peace be with you; I AM THE LIVING GOD, be not afraid.”
24.37: And they were alarmed and were in terror, for they thought that they were seeing a ghost.
24.38: And Yeshua said to them, “Why are you shaken, and why do imaginations arise in your hearts?”
24.39: “See my hands and my feet, that it is I; touch me and know that a ghost does not have flesh and bones, as you see that I have.”
24.40: And when he had said these things, he showed them his hands and his feet.
24.41: Even until this moment they did not believe because of their joy, and they were awe-stricken. He said to them, “Do you have anything here to eat?”
24.42: So they gave him a piece of roasted fish and some honeycomb.
24.43: And he took and he ate in their sight.
24.44: And he said to them, “These are the words that I spoke with you when I was with you, that everything that is written about me in The Law of Moses and in The Prophets and in The Psalms must be fulfilled.”
24.45: Then he opened their mind to understand the scriptures.
24.46: And he said to them, “Thus it is written: and thus it was right for The Messiah to suffer and to arise from the grave the third day,
24.47: And that conversion to the forgiveness of sins would be proclaimed in his name in all the nations, and the beginning would be at Jerusalem.
24.48: And you are witnesses of these things.
24.49: And I shall send upon you The Promise of my Father; but you stay in the city of Jerusalem until you shall be clothed in power from on high.”
24.50: And he brought them unto Bethany and he lifted his hands and he blessed them.
24.51: And it was that as he blessed them, he was separated from them, and he ascended to Heaven.
24.52: But they worshiped him and they returned to Jerusalem in great joy.
24.53: And they were in The Temple at all times, praising and blessing God. Amen.
